_atom_sites.solution_primary.name: _atom_sites.solution_primary
_atom_sites.solution_primary.description:
This code identifies the method used to locate the initial
               atom sites.

               *** This data item would not in general be used in a
               macromolecular data block. ***

_atom_sites.solution_secondary.name: _atom_sites.solution_secondary
_atom_sites.solution_secondary.description:
This code identifies the method used to locate the
              non-hydrogen-atom sites not found by
              _atom_sites.solution_primary.

               *** This data item would not in general be used in a
               macromolecular data block. ***

_atom_sites.solution_hydrogens.name: _atom_sites.solution_hydrogens
_atom_sites.solution_hydrogens.description:
This code identifies the method used to locate the
               hydrogen atoms.

               *** This data item would not in general be used in a
               macromolecular data block. ***

_atom_sites_alt.details.name: _atom_sites_alt.details
_atom_sites_alt.details.description:
A description of special aspects of the modelling of atoms in
               alternative conformations.

_atom_sites_alt_ens.details.name: _atom_sites_alt_ens.details
_atom_sites_alt_ens.details.description:
A description of special aspects of the ensemble structure
               generated from atoms with various alternative IDs.

_atom_sites_alt_gen.alt_id.name: _atom_sites_alt_gen.alt_id
_atom_sites_alt_gen.alt_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _atom_sites_alt.id in the
               ATOM_SITES_ALT category.

_atom_sites_alt_gen.ens_id.name: _atom_sites_alt_gen.ens_id
_atom_sites_alt_gen.ens_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _atom_sites_alt_ens.id in the
               ATOM_SITES_ALT_ENS category.

_atom_sites_footnote.text.name: _atom_sites_footnote.text
_atom_sites_footnote.text.description:
The text of the footnote. Footnotes are used to describe
               an atom site or a group of atom sites in the ATOM_SITE list.

               For example, footnotes may be used to indicate atoms for which
               the electron density is very weak, or atoms for which static
               disorder has been modelled.

_atom_type.analytical_mass_percent.name: _atom_type.analytical_mass_percent
_atom_type.analytical_mass_percent.description: "              Mass percentage of this atom type derived from chemical analysis."

_atom_type.description.name: _atom_type.description
_atom_type.description.description:
A description of the atom(s) designated by this atom type. In
               most cases, this is the element name and oxidation state of
               a single atom species. For disordered or nonstoichiometric
               structures it will describe a combination of atom species.

_atom_type.number_in_cell.name: _atom_type.number_in_cell
_atom_type.number_in_cell.description: "              Total number of atoms of this atom type in the unit cell."

_atom_type.oxidation_number.name: _atom_type.oxidation_number
_atom_type.oxidation_number.description: "              Formal oxidation state of this atom type in the structure."

_atom_type.radius_bond.name: _atom_type.radius_bond
_atom_type.radius_bond.description:
The effective intramolecular bonding radius in angstroms
               of this atom type.

_atom_type.radius_contact.name: _atom_type.radius_contact
_atom_type.radius_contact.description:
The effective intermolecular bonding radius in angstroms
               of this atom type.

_atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_a1.name: _atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_a1
_atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_a1.description:
The Cromer-Mann scattering-factor coefficient a1 used to
               calculate the scattering factors for this atom type.

               Ref: International Tables for X-ray Crystallography (1974).
                   Vol. IV, Table 2.2B
                or: International Tables for Crystallography  (2004). Vol. C,
                    Tables 6.1.1.4 and 6.1.1.5.

_atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_b2.description:
The Cromer-Mann scattering-factor coefficient b2 used to
               calculate the scattering factors for this atom type.

               Ref: International Tables for X-ray Crystallography (1974).
                   Vol. IV, Table 2.2B
                or: International Tables for Crystallography  (2004). Vol. C,
                    Tables 6.1.1.4 and 6.1.1.5.

_atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_b3.name: _atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_b3
_atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_b3.description:
The Cromer-Mann scattering-factor coefficient b3 used to
               calculate the scattering factors for this atom type.

               Ref: International Tables for X-ray Crystallography (1974).
                   Vol. IV, Table 2.2B
                or: International Tables for Crystallography  (2004). Vol. C,
                    Tables 6.1.1.4 and 6.1.1.5.

_atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_b4.name: _atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_b4
_atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_b4.description:
The Cromer-Mann scattering-factor coefficient b4 used to
               calculate the scattering factors for this atom type.

               Ref: International Tables for X-ray Crystallography (1974).
                   Vol. IV, Table 2.2B
                or: International Tables for Crystallography  (2004). Vol. C,
                    Tables 6.1.1.4 and 6.1.1.5.

_atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_c.name: _atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_c
_atom_type.scat_Cromer_Mann_c.description:
The Cromer-Mann scattering-factor coefficient c used to
               calculate the scattering factors for this atom type.

               Ref: International Tables for X-ray Crystallography (1974).
                   Vol. IV, Table 2.2B
                or: International Tables for Crystallography  (2004). Vol. C,
                    Tables 6.1.1.4 and 6.1.1.5.

_atom_type.scat_dispersion_imag.name: _atom_type.scat_dispersion_imag
_atom_type.scat_dispersion_imag.description:
The imaginary component of the anomalous-dispersion
               scattering factor, f'', in electrons for this atom type and
               the radiation identified by _diffrn_radiation_wavelength.id.

_atom_type.scat_dispersion_real.name: _atom_type.scat_dispersion_real
_atom_type.scat_dispersion_real.description:
The real component of the anomalous-dispersion
               scattering factor, f', in electrons for this atom type and
               the radiation identified by _diffrn_radiation_wavelength.id.

_atom_type.scat_length_neutron.name: _atom_type.scat_length_neutron
_atom_type.scat_length_neutron.description:
The bound coherent scattering length in femtometres for the
               atom type at the isotopic composition used for the diffraction
               experiment.

_atom_type.scat_source.name: _atom_type.scat_source
_atom_type.scat_source.description:
Reference to the source of the scattering factors or scattering
               lengths used for this atom type.

_atom_type.scat_versus_stol_list.name: _atom_type.scat_versus_stol_list
_atom_type.scat_versus_stol_list.description:
A table of scattering factors as a function of sin theta over
               lambda. This table should be well commented to indicate the
               items present. Regularly formatted lists are strongly
               recommended.

_audit.creation_date.name: _audit.creation_date
_audit.creation_date.description:
A date that the data block was created. The date format is
               yyyy-mm-dd.

_audit.creation_method.name: _audit.creation_method
_audit.creation_method.description: "              A description of how data were entered into the data block."

_audit.revision_id.name: _audit.revision_id
_audit.revision_id.description:
The value of _audit.revision_id must uniquely identify a record
               in the AUDIT list.

_audit.update_record.name: _audit.update_record
_audit.update_record.description:
A record of any changes to the data block. The update format is
               a date (yyyy-mm-dd) followed by a description of the changes.
               The latest update entry is added to the bottom of this record.

_audit_author.address.name: _audit_author.address
_audit_author.address.description:
The address of an author of this data block. If there are
               multiple authors, _audit_author.address is looped with
               _audit_author.name.
examples:
Department
                                  Institute
                                  Street
                                  City and postcode
                                  COUNTRY

_audit_author.name.name: _audit_author.name
_audit_author.name.description:
The name of an author of this data block. If there are multiple
               authors, _audit_author.name is looped with _audit_author.address.
               The family name(s), followed by a comma and including any
               dynastic components, precedes the first name(s) or initial(s).

_audit_conform.dict_location.name: _audit_conform.dict_location
_audit_conform.dict_location.description:
A file name or uniform resource locator (URL) for the
               dictionary to which the current data block conforms.

_audit_conform.dict_name.name: _audit_conform.dict_name
_audit_conform.dict_name.description:
The string identifying the highest-level dictionary defining
               data names used in this file.

_audit_conform.dict_version.name: _audit_conform.dict_version
_audit_conform.dict_version.description:
The version number of the dictionary to which the current
               data block conforms.

_audit_contact_author.address.name: _audit_contact_author.address
_audit_contact_author.address.description:
The mailing address of the author of the data block to whom
               correspondence should be addressed.
examples:
Department
                                  Institute
                                  Street
                                  City and postcode
                                  COUNTRY

_audit_contact_author.email.name: _audit_contact_author.email
_audit_contact_author.email.description:
The electronic mail address of the author of the data block to
               whom correspondence should be addressed, in a form recognizable
               to international networks. The format of e-mail
               addresses is given in Section 3.4, Address Specification, of
               Internet Message Format, RFC 2822, P. Resnick (Editor),
               Network Standards Group, April 2001.

_audit_contact_author.fax.name: _audit_contact_author.fax
_audit_contact_author.fax.description:
The facsimile telephone number of the author of the data
               block to whom correspondence should be addressed.

               The recommended style starts with the international dialing
               prefix, followed by the area code in parentheses, followed by the
               local number with no spaces.

_audit_contact_author.name.name: _audit_contact_author.name
_audit_contact_author.name.description:
The name of the author of the data block to whom correspondence
               should be addressed.

               The family name(s), followed by a comma and including any
               dynastic components, precedes the first name(s) or initial(s).

_audit_contact_author.phone.name: _audit_contact_author.phone
_audit_contact_author.phone.description:
The telephone number of the author of the data block to whom
               correspondence should be addressed.

               The recommended style starts with the international dialing
               prefix, followed by the area code in parentheses, followed by the
               local number and any extension number prefixed by 'x',
               with no spaces.

_cell.angle_alpha.name: _cell.angle_alpha
_cell.angle_alpha.description: "              Unit-cell angle alpha of the reported structure in degrees."

_cell.angle_alpha_esd.name: _cell.angle_alpha_esd
_cell.angle_alpha_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _cell.angle_alpha.

_cell.angle_beta.name: _cell.angle_beta
_cell.angle_beta.description: "              Unit-cell angle beta of the reported structure in degrees."

_cell.angle_beta_esd.name: _cell.angle_beta_esd
_cell.angle_beta_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _cell.angle_beta.

_cell.angle_gamma.name: _cell.angle_gamma
_cell.angle_gamma.description: "              Unit-cell angle gamma of the reported structure in degrees."

_cell.angle_gamma_esd.name: _cell.angle_gamma_esd
_cell.angle_gamma_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _cell.angle_gamma.

_cell.entry_id.name: _cell.entry_id
_cell.entry_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category."

_cell.details.name: _cell.details
_cell.details.description:
A description of special aspects of the cell choice, noting
               possible alternative settings.
examples: "standard setting from 45 deg rotation around c"

_cell.formula_units_Z.name: _cell.formula_units_Z
_cell.formula_units_Z.description:
The number of the formula units in the unit cell as specified
               by _chemical_formula.structural, _chemical_formula.moiety or
               _chemical_formula.sum.

_cell.length_a.name: _cell.length_a
_cell.length_a.description:
Unit-cell length a corresponding to the structure reported in
              angstroms.

_cell.length_a_esd.name: _cell.length_a_esd
_cell.length_a_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _cell.length_a.

_cell.length_b.name: _cell.length_b
_cell.length_b.description:
Unit-cell length b corresponding to the structure reported in
               angstroms.

_cell.length_b_esd.name: _cell.length_b_esd
_cell.length_b_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _cell.length_b.

_cell.length_c.name: _cell.length_c
_cell.length_c.description:
Unit-cell length c corresponding to the structure reported in
              angstroms.

_cell.length_c_esd.name: _cell.length_c_esd
_cell.length_c_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _cell.length_c.

_cell.volume.name: _cell.volume
_cell.volume.description:
Cell volume V in angstroms cubed.

               V = a b c (1 - cos^2^~alpha~ - cos^2^~beta~ - cos^2^~gamma~
                          + 2 cos~alpha~ cos~beta~ cos~gamma~)^1/2^

               a     = _cell.length_a
               b     = _cell.length_b
               c     = _cell.length_c
               alpha = _cell.angle_alpha
               beta  = _cell.angle_beta
               gamma = _cell.angle_gamma

_cell.volume_esd.name: _cell.volume_esd
_cell.volume_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _cell.volume.

_cell.Z_PDB.name: _cell.Z_PDB
_cell.Z_PDB.description:
The number of the polymeric chains in a unit cell. In the case
               of heteropolymers, Z is the number of occurrences of the most
               populous chain.

               This data item is provided for compatibility with the original
               Protein Data Bank format, and only for that purpose.

_cell_measurement.entry_id.name: _cell_measurement.entry_id
_cell_measurement.entry_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category."

_cell_measurement.pressure.name: _cell_measurement.pressure
_cell_measurement.pressure.description:
The pressure in kilopascals at which the unit-cell parameters
               were measured (not the pressure at which the sample was
               synthesized).

_cell_measurement.pressure_esd.name: _cell_measurement.pressure_esd
_cell_measurement.pressure_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _cell_measurement.pressure.

_cell_measurement.radiation.name: _cell_measurement.radiation
_cell_measurement.radiation.description:
Description of the radiation used to measure the unit-cell data.
               See also _cell_measurement.wavelength.

_cell_measurement.reflns_used.name: _cell_measurement.reflns_used
_cell_measurement.reflns_used.description:
The total number of reflections used to determine the unit cell.
               These reflections may be specified as CELL_MEASUREMENT_REFLN
               data items.

_cell_measurement.temp.name: _cell_measurement.temp
_cell_measurement.temp.description:
The temperature in kelvins at which the unit-cell parameters
               were measured (not the temperature of synthesis).

_cell_measurement.temp_esd.name: _cell_measurement.temp_esd
_cell_measurement.temp_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _cell_measurement.temp.

_cell_measurement.theta_max.name: _cell_measurement.theta_max
_cell_measurement.theta_max.description:
The maximum theta angle of reflections used to measure
               the unit cell in degrees.

_cell_measurement.theta_min.name: _cell_measurement.theta_min
_cell_measurement.theta_min.description:
The minimum theta angle of reflections used to measure
               the unit cell in degrees.

_cell_measurement.wavelength.name: _cell_measurement.wavelength
_cell_measurement.wavelength.description:
The wavelength in angstroms of the radiation used to measure
               the unit cell. If this is not specified, the wavelength is
               assumed to be that specified in the category
               DIFFRN_RADIATION_WAVELENGTH.

_cell_measurement_refln.index_h.name: _cell_measurement_refln.index_h
_cell_measurement_refln.index_h.description:
Miller index h of a reflection used for measurement of the unit
               cell.

_cell_measurement_refln.index_k.name: _cell_measurement_refln.index_k
_cell_measurement_refln.index_k.description:
Miller index k of a reflection used for measurement of the unit
               cell.

_cell_measurement_refln.index_l.name: _cell_measurement_refln.index_l
_cell_measurement_refln.index_l.description:
Miller index l of a reflection used for measurement of the unit
               cell.

_cell_measurement_refln.theta.name: _cell_measurement_refln.theta
_cell_measurement_refln.theta.description:
Theta angle for a reflection used for measurement of
               the unit cell in degrees.

_chem_comp.formula.name: _chem_comp.formula
_chem_comp.formula.description:
The formula for the chemical component. Formulae are written
               according to the following rules:

               (1) Only recognized element symbols may be used.

               (2) Each element symbol is followed by a 'count' number. A count
                  of '1' may be omitted.

               (3) A space or parenthesis must separate each cluster of
                  (element symbol + count), but in general parentheses are
                  not used.

               (4) The order of elements depends on whether carbon is
                  present or not. If carbon is present, the order should be:
                  C, then H, then the other elements in alphabetical order
                  of their symbol. If carbon is not present, the elements
                  are listed purely in alphabetic order of their symbol. This
                  is the 'Hill' system used by Chemical Abstracts.

_chem_comp.formula_weight.name: _chem_comp.formula_weight
_chem_comp.formula_weight.description: "              Formula mass in daltons of the chemical component."

_chem_comp.model_details.name: _chem_comp.model_details
_chem_comp.model_details.description:
A description of special aspects of the generation of the
               coordinates for the model of the component.

_chem_comp.model_erf.name: _chem_comp.model_erf
_chem_comp.model_erf.description:
A pointer to an external reference file from which the atomic
               description of the component is taken.

_chem_comp.model_source.name: _chem_comp.model_source
_chem_comp.model_source.description: "              The source of the coordinates for the model of the component."

_chem_comp.mon_nstd_class.name: _chem_comp.mon_nstd_class
_chem_comp.mon_nstd_class.description:
A description of the class of a nonstandard monomer if the
               nonstandard monomer represents a modification of a
               standard monomer.

_chem_comp.mon_nstd_details.name: _chem_comp.mon_nstd_details
_chem_comp.mon_nstd_details.description: "              A description of special details of a nonstandard monomer."

_chem_comp.mon_nstd_flag.name: _chem_comp.mon_nstd_flag
_chem_comp.mon_nstd_flag.description:
'yes' indicates that this is a 'standard' monomer, 'no'
               indicates that it is 'nonstandard'. Nonstandard monomers
               should be described in more detail using the
               _chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent, _chem_comp.mon_nstd_class and
               _chem_comp.mon_nstd_details data items.

_chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent.name: _chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent
_chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent.description:
The name of the parent monomer of the nonstandard monomer,
               if the nonstandard monomer represents a modification of a
               standard monomer.

_chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent_comp_id.name: _chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent_comp_id
_chem_comp.mon_nstd_parent_comp_id.description:
The identifier for the parent component of the nonstandard
               component. May be be a comma separated list if this component
               is derived from multiple components.

               Items in this indirectly point to _chem_comp.id in
               the CHEM_COMP category.

_chem_comp.name.name: _chem_comp.name
_chem_comp.name.description: "              The full name of the component."

_chem_comp.number_atoms_all.name: _chem_comp.number_atoms_all
_chem_comp.number_atoms_all.description: "              The total number of atoms in the component."

_chem_comp.number_atoms_nh.name: _chem_comp.number_atoms_nh
_chem_comp.number_atoms_nh.description: "              The number of non-hydrogen atoms in the component."

_chem_comp.one_letter_code.name: _chem_comp.one_letter_code
_chem_comp.one_letter_code.description:
For standard polymer components, the one-letter code for
               the component.   For non-standard polymer components, the
               one-letter code for parent component if this exists;
               otherwise, the one-letter code should be given as 'X'.

               Components that derived from multiple parents components
               are described by a sequence of one-letter-codes.

_chem_comp.three_letter_code.name: _chem_comp.three_letter_code
_chem_comp.three_letter_code.description:
For standard polymer components, the common three-letter code for
               the component.   Non-standard polymer components and non-polymer
               components are also assigned three-letter-codes.

               For ambiguous polymer components three-letter code should
               be given as 'UNK'.  Ambiguous ions are assigned the code 'UNX'.
               Ambiguous non-polymer components are assigned the code 'UNL'.

_chem_comp_angle.atom_id_1.name: _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_1
_chem_comp_angle.atom_id_1.description:
The ID of the first of the three atoms that define the angle.

               This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the
               CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_angle.atom_id_2.name: _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_2
_chem_comp_angle.atom_id_2.description:
The ID of the second of the three atoms that define the angle.
               The second atom is taken to be the apex of the angle.

               This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the
               CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_angle.atom_id_3.name: _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_3
_chem_comp_angle.atom_id_3.description:
The ID of the third of the three atoms that define the angle.

               This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the
               CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_angle.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_angle.comp_id
_chem_comp_angle.comp_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP
               category.

_chem_comp_angle.value_angle.name: _chem_comp_angle.value_angle
_chem_comp_angle.value_angle.description:
The value that should be taken as the target value for the angle
               associated with the specified atoms, expressed in degrees.

_chem_comp_angle.value_angle_esd.name: _chem_comp_angle.value_angle_esd
_chem_comp_angle.value_angle_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _chem_comp_angle.value_angle.

_chem_comp_angle.value_dist.name: _chem_comp_angle.value_dist
_chem_comp_angle.value_dist.description:
The value that should be taken as the target value for the angle
               associated with the specified atoms, expressed as the distance
               between the atoms specified by _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_1 and
               _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_3.

_chem_comp_angle.value_dist_esd.name: _chem_comp_angle.value_dist_esd
_chem_comp_angle.value_dist_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _chem_comp_angle.value_dist.

_chem_comp_atom.alt_atom_id.name: _chem_comp_atom.alt_atom_id
_chem_comp_atom.alt_atom_id.description:
An alternative identifier for the atom. This data item would be
               used in cases where alternative nomenclatures exist for labelling
               atoms in a group.

_chem_comp_atom.charge.name: _chem_comp_atom.charge
_chem_comp_atom.charge.description:
The net integer charge assigned to this atom. This is the
               formal charge assignment normally found in chemical diagrams.

_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x.name: _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x
_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x.description:
The x component of the coordinates for this atom in this
               component specified as orthogonal angstroms. The choice of
               reference axis frame for the coordinates is arbitrary.

               The set of coordinates input for the entity here is intended to
               correspond to the atomic model used to generate restraints for
               structure refinement, not to atom sites in the ATOM_SITE
               list.

_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x_esd.name: _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x_esd
_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_x.

_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y.name: _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y
_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y.description:
The y component of the coordinates for this atom in this
               component specified as orthogonal angstroms. The choice of
               reference axis frame for the coordinates is arbitrary.

               The set of coordinates input for the entity here is intended to
               correspond to the atomic model used to generate restraints for
               structure refinement, not to atom sites in the ATOM_SITE
               list.

_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y_esd.name: _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y_esd
_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_y.

_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z.name: _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z
_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z.description:
The z component of the coordinates for this atom in this
               component specified as orthogonal angstroms. The choice of
               reference axis frame for the coordinates is arbitrary.

               The set of coordinates input for the entity here is intended to
               correspond to the atomic model used to generate restraints for
               structure refinement, not to atom sites in the ATOM_SITE
               list.

_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z_esd.name: _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z_esd
_chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _chem_comp_atom.model_Cartn_z.

_chem_comp_atom.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_atom.comp_id
_chem_comp_atom.comp_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP
               category.

_chem_comp_atom.partial_charge.name: _chem_comp_atom.partial_charge
_chem_comp_atom.partial_charge.description: "              The partial charge assigned to this atom."

_chem_comp_atom.substruct_code.name: _chem_comp_atom.substruct_code
_chem_comp_atom.substruct_code.description:
This data item assigns the atom to a substructure of the
               component, if appropriate.

_chem_comp_atom.type_symbol.name: _chem_comp_atom.type_symbol
_chem_comp_atom.type_symbol.description:
The code used to identify the atom species representing
               this atom type. Normally this code is the element
               symbol.

_chem_comp_bond.atom_id_1.name: _chem_comp_bond.atom_id_1
_chem_comp_bond.atom_id_1.description:
The ID of the first of the two atoms that define the bond.

               This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the
               CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_bond.atom_id_2.name: _chem_comp_bond.atom_id_2
_chem_comp_bond.atom_id_2.description:
The ID of the second of the two atoms that define the bond.

               This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the
               CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_bond.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_bond.comp_id
_chem_comp_bond.comp_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP
               category.

_chem_comp_bond.value_order.name: _chem_comp_bond.value_order
_chem_comp_bond.value_order.description:
The value that should be taken as the target for the chemical
               bond associated with the specified atoms, expressed as a bond
               order.

_chem_comp_bond.value_dist.name: _chem_comp_bond.value_dist
_chem_comp_bond.value_dist.description:
The value that should be taken as the target for the chemical
               bond associated with the specified atoms, expressed as a
               distance.

_chem_comp_bond.value_dist_esd.name: _chem_comp_bond.value_dist_esd
_chem_comp_bond.value_dist_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _chem_comp_bond.value_dist.

_chem_comp_chir.atom_id.name: _chem_comp_chir.atom_id
_chem_comp_chir.atom_id.description:
The ID of the atom that is a chiral centre.

               This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the
               CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_chir.atom_config.name: _chem_comp_chir.atom_config
_chem_comp_chir.atom_config.description: "              The chiral configuration of the atom that is a chiral centre."

_chem_comp_chir.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_chir.comp_id
_chem_comp_chir.comp_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP
               category.

_chem_comp_chir.number_atoms_all.name: _chem_comp_chir.number_atoms_all
_chem_comp_chir.number_atoms_all.description:
The total number of atoms bonded to the atom specified by
               _chem_comp_chir.atom_id.

_chem_comp_chir.number_atoms_nh.name: _chem_comp_chir.number_atoms_nh
_chem_comp_chir.number_atoms_nh.description:
The number of non-hydrogen atoms bonded to the atom specified by
               _chem_comp_chir.atom_id.

_chem_comp_chir.volume_flag.name: _chem_comp_chir.volume_flag
_chem_comp_chir.volume_flag.description:
A flag to indicate whether a chiral volume should match the
               standard value in both magnitude and sign, or in magnitude only.

_chem_comp_chir.volume_three.name: _chem_comp_chir.volume_three
_chem_comp_chir.volume_three.description:
The chiral volume, V~c~, for chiral centres that involve a chiral
               atom bonded to three non-hydrogen atoms and one hydrogen atom.

               V~c~ = V1 * (V2 X V3)

               V1 = the vector distance from the atom specified by
                    _chem_comp_chir.atom_id to the first atom in the
                    CHEM_COMP_CHIR_ATOM list
               V2 = the vector distance from the atom specified by
                    _chem_comp_chir.atom_id to the second atom in the
                    CHEM_COMP_CHIR_ATOM list
               V3 = the vector distance from the atom specified by
                    _chem_comp_chir.atom_id to the third atom in the
                    CHEM_COMP_CHIR_ATOM list
               *  = the vector dot product
               X  = the vector cross product

_chem_comp_chir.volume_three_esd.name: _chem_comp_chir.volume_three_esd
_chem_comp_chir.volume_three_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _chem_comp_chir.volume_three.

_chem_comp_chir_atom.atom_id.name: _chem_comp_chir_atom.atom_id
_chem_comp_chir_atom.atom_id.description:
The ID of an atom bonded to the chiral atom.

               This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the
               CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_chir_atom.chir_id.name: _chem_comp_chir_atom.chir_id
_chem_comp_chir_atom.chir_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_chir.id in the
               CHEM_COMP_CHIR category.

_chem_comp_chir_atom.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_chir_atom.comp_id

               CHEM_COMP category.

_chem_comp_chir_atom.dev.name: _chem_comp_chir_atom.dev
_chem_comp_chir_atom.dev.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of the position of this atom from the plane defined by
               all of the atoms in the plane.

_chem_comp_link.link_id.name: _chem_comp_link.link_id
_chem_comp_link.link_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_link.id in the
               CHEM_LINK category.

_chem_comp_link.details.name: _chem_comp_link.details
_chem_comp_link.details.description:
A description of special aspects of a link between
               chemical components in the structure.

_chem_comp_link.type_comp_1.description:
The type of the first of the two components joined by the
               link.

               category.

_chem_comp_link.type_comp_2.name: _chem_comp_link.type_comp_2
_chem_comp_link.type_comp_2.description:
The type of the second of the two components joined by the

               This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.type in the CHEM_COMP
               category.

_chem_comp_plane.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_plane.comp_id
_chem_comp_plane.comp_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP
               category.

_chem_comp_plane.number_atoms_all.name: _chem_comp_plane.number_atoms_all
_chem_comp_plane.number_atoms_all.description: "              The total number of atoms in the plane."

_chem_comp_plane.number_atoms_nh.name: _chem_comp_plane.number_atoms_nh
_chem_comp_plane.number_atoms_nh.description: "              The number of non-hydrogen atoms in the plane."

_chem_comp_plane_atom.atom_id.name: _chem_comp_plane_atom.atom_id
_chem_comp_plane_atom.atom_id.description:

_chem_comp_plane_atom.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_plane_atom.comp_id
_chem_comp_plane_atom.comp_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP
               category.

_chem_comp_plane_atom.plane_id.name: _chem_comp_plane_atom.plane_id
_chem_comp_plane_atom.plane_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_plane.id in the
               CHEM_COMP_PLANE category.

_chem_comp_plane_atom.dist_esd.name: _chem_comp_plane_atom.dist_esd
_chem_comp_plane_atom.dist_esd.description:
This data item is the standard deviation of the
               out-of-plane distance for this atom.

_chem_comp_tor.atom_id_1.name: _chem_comp_tor.atom_id_1
_chem_comp_tor.atom_id_1.description:
The ID of the first of the four atoms that define the torsion
               angle.

               This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the
               CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_tor.atom_id_2.name: _chem_comp_tor.atom_id_2
_chem_comp_tor.atom_id_2.description:
The ID of the second of the four atoms that define the torsion

               CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_tor.atom_id_3.name: _chem_comp_tor.atom_id_3
_chem_comp_tor.atom_id_3.description:
The ID of the third of the four atoms that define the torsion
               angle.

               This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the
               CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_tor.atom_id_4.name: _chem_comp_tor.atom_id_4
_chem_comp_tor.atom_id_4.description:
The ID of the fourth of the four atoms that define the torsion
               angle.

               This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.atom_id in the
               CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_tor.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_tor.comp_id
_chem_comp_tor.comp_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp.id in the CHEM_COMP
               category.

_chem_comp_tor_value.comp_id.name: _chem_comp_tor_value.comp_id
_chem_comp_tor_value.comp_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_atom.comp_id in the
               CHEM_COMP_ATOM category.

_chem_comp_tor_value.tor_id.name: _chem_comp_tor_value.tor_id
_chem_comp_tor_value.tor_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_comp_tor.id in the
               CHEM_COMP_TOR category.

_chem_comp_tor_value.angle.name: _chem_comp_tor_value.angle
_chem_comp_tor_value.angle.description:
A value that should be taken as a potential target value for the
               torsion angle associated with the specified atoms, expressed in
               degrees.

_chem_comp_tor_value.angle_esd.name: _chem_comp_tor_value.angle_esd
_chem_comp_tor_value.angle_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _chem_comp_tor_value.angle.

_chem_comp_tor_value.dist.name: _chem_comp_tor_value.dist
_chem_comp_tor_value.dist.description:
A value that should be taken as a potential target value for the
               torsion angle associated with the specified atoms, expressed as
               the distance between the atoms specified by
               _chem_comp_tor.atom_id_1 and _chem_comp_tor.atom_id_4 in the
               referenced record in the CHEM_COMP_TOR list. Note that the
               torsion angle cannot be fully specified by a distance (for
               instance, a torsion angle of -60  degree will yield the same
               distance as a 60 degree angle). However, the distance
               specification can be useful for refinement in situations
               in which the angle is already close to the desired value.

_chem_comp_tor_value.dist_esd.name: _chem_comp_tor_value.dist_esd
_chem_comp_tor_value.dist_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _chem_comp_tor_value.dist.

_chem_link.details.name: _chem_link.details
_chem_link.details.description:
A description of special aspects of a link between
               chemical components in the structure.

_chem_link_angle.atom_1_comp_id.name: _chem_link_angle.atom_1_comp_id
_chem_link_angle.atom_1_comp_id.description:
This data item indicates whether atom 1 is found in the first
               or the second of the two components connected by the link.

_chem_link_angle.atom_2_comp_id.name: _chem_link_angle.atom_2_comp_id
_chem_link_angle.atom_2_comp_id.description:
This data item indicates whether atom 2 is found in the first
               or the second of the two components connected by the link.

_chem_link_angle.atom_3_comp_id.name: _chem_link_angle.atom_3_comp_id
_chem_link_angle.atom_3_comp_id.description:
This data item indicates whether atom 3 is found in the first
               or the second of the two components connected by the link.

_chem_link_angle.atom_id_1.name: _chem_link_angle.atom_id_1
_chem_link_angle.atom_id_1.description:
The ID of the first of the three atoms that define the angle.

               An atom with this ID must exist in the component of the type
               specified by _chem_comp_link.type_comp_1 (or
               _chem_comp_link.type_comp_2, where the appropriate data item
               is indicated by the value of _chem_comp_angle.atom_1_comp_id).

_chem_link_angle.atom_id_2.name: _chem_link_angle.atom_id_2
_chem_link_angle.atom_id_2.description:
The ID of the second of the three atoms that define the angle.
               The second atom is taken to be the apex of the angle.

               An atom with this ID must exist in the component of the type
               specified by _chem_comp_link.type_comp_1 (or
               _chem_comp_link.type_comp_2, where the appropriate data item
               is indicated by the value of _chem_comp_angle.atom_2_comp_id).

_chem_link_angle.atom_id_3.name: _chem_link_angle.atom_id_3
_chem_link_angle.atom_id_3.description:
The ID of the third of the three atoms that define the angle.

               An atom with this ID must exist in the component of the type
               specified by _chem_comp_link.type_comp_1 (or
               _chem_comp_link.type_comp_2, where the appropriate data item
              is indicated by the value of _chem_comp_angle.atom_3_comp_id).

_chem_link_angle.link_id.name: _chem_link_angle.link_id
_chem_link_angle.link_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_link.id in the CHEM_LINK
               category.

_chem_link_angle.value_angle.name: _chem_link_angle.value_angle
_chem_link_angle.value_angle.description:
The value that should be taken as the target value for the angle
               associated with the specified atoms, expressed in degrees.

_chem_link_angle.value_angle_esd.name: _chem_link_angle.value_angle_esd
_chem_link_angle.value_angle_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _chem_link_angle.value_angle.

_chem_link_angle.value_dist.name: _chem_link_angle.value_dist
_chem_link_angle.value_dist.description:
The value that should be taken as the target value for the angle
               associated with the specified atoms, expressed as the distance
               between the atoms specified by _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_1 and
               _chem_comp_angle.atom_id_3.

_chem_link_angle.value_dist_esd.name: _chem_link_angle.value_dist_esd
_chem_link_angle.value_dist_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _chem_comp_angle.value_dist.

_chem_link_bond.atom_1_comp_id.name: _chem_link_bond.atom_1_comp_id
_chem_link_bond.atom_1_comp_id.description:
This data item indicates whether atom 1 is found in the first
               or the second of the two components connected by the link.

_chem_link_bond.atom_2_comp_id.name: _chem_link_bond.atom_2_comp_id
_chem_link_bond.atom_2_comp_id.description:
This data item indicates whether atom 2 is found in the first
               or the second of the two chemical components connected by
               the link.

_chem_link_bond.atom_id_1.name: _chem_link_bond.atom_id_1
_chem_link_bond.atom_id_1.description:
The ID of the first of the two atoms that define the bond.

               As this data item does not point to a specific atom in a
               specific chemical component, it is not a child in the
               linkage sense.

_chem_link_bond.atom_id_2.name: _chem_link_bond.atom_id_2
_chem_link_bond.atom_id_2.description:
The ID of the second of the two atoms that define the bond.

               As this data item does not point to a specific atom in a
               specific component, it is not a child in the linkage sense.

_chem_link_bond.link_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_link.id in the CHEM_LINK
               category.

_chem_link_bond.value_dist.description:
The value that should be taken as the target for the chemical
               bond associated with the specified atoms, expressed as a
               distance.

_chem_link_bond.value_dist_esd.name: _chem_link_bond.value_dist_esd
_chem_link_bond.value_dist_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _chem_link_bond.value_dist.

_chem_link_bond.value_order.description:
The value that should be taken as the target for the chemical

_chem_link_chir.atom_comp_id.description:

               first or the second of the two components connected by the
               link.

_chem_link_chir.atom_id.description:
The ID of the atom that is a chiral centre.

_chem_link_chir.atom_config.description: "              The chiral configuration of the atom that is a chiral centre."

A flag to indicate whether a chiral volume should match the
               standard value in both magnitude and sign, or in magnitude only.

_chem_link_chir.volume_three.description:
The chiral volume, V(c), for chiral centres that involve a chiral
               atom bonded to three non-hydrogen atoms and one hydrogen atom.

               V~c~ = V1 * (V2 X V3)

               V1 = the vector distance from the atom specified by
                    _chem_link_chir.atom_id to the first atom in the
                    CHEM_LINK_CHIR_ATOM list
               V2 = the vector distance from the atom specified by
                    _chem_link_chir.atom_id to the second atom in the
                    CHEM_LINK_CHIR_ATOM list
               V3 = the vector distance from the atom specified by
                    _chem_link_chir.atom_id to the third atom in the
                    CHEM_LINK_CHIR_ATOM list
               *  = the vector dot product
               X  = the vector cross product

               of _chem_link_chir.volume_three.

_chem_link_tor.atom_id_2.name: _chem_link_tor.atom_id_2

_chem_link_tor.atom_id_4.name: _chem_link_tor.atom_id_4

_chem_link_tor_value.tor_id.description:

_chem_link_tor_value.angle.description:
A value that should be taken as a potential target value for the
               torsion angle associated with the specified atoms, expressed in

               of _chem_link_tor_value.angle.

A value that should be taken as a potential target value for the
               torsion angle associated with the specified atoms, expressed as
               the distance between the atoms specified by
               _chem_link_tor.atom_id_1 and _chem_link_tor.atom_id_4 in the
               referenced record in the CHEM_LINK_TOR list. Note that the
               torsion angle cannot be fully specified by a distance (for
               instance, a torsion angle of -60 degree will yield the same
               distance as a 60 degree angle). However, the distance
               specification can be useful for refinement in situations in
               which the angle is already close to the desired value.

_chem_link_tor_value.dist_esd.name: _chem_link_tor_value.dist_esd
_chem_link_tor_value.dist_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _chem_link_tor_value.dist.

_chemical.entry_id.name: _chemical.entry_id
_chemical.entry_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category."

_chemical.compound_source.name: _chemical.compound_source
_chemical.compound_source.description:
Description of the source of the compound under study, or of the
               parent  molecule if a simple derivative is studied. This includes
               the place of discovery for minerals or the actual source of a
               natural product.
examples: "Extracted from the bark of Cinchona Naturalis"

_chemical.melting_point.name: _chemical.melting_point
_chemical.melting_point.description:
The temperature in kelvins at which the crystalline solid changes
               to a liquid.

_chemical.name_common.name: _chemical.name_common
_chemical.name_common.description: "              Trivial name by which the compound is commonly known."

_chemical.name_mineral.name: _chemical.name_mineral
_chemical.name_mineral.description:
Mineral name accepted by the International Mineralogical
               Association. Use only for natural minerals. See also
               _chemical.compound_source.

_chemical.name_structure_type.name: _chemical.name_structure_type
_chemical.name_structure_type.description:
Commonly used structure-type name. Usually only applied to
               minerals or inorganic compounds.

_chemical.name_systematic.name: _chemical.name_systematic
_chemical.name_systematic.description: "              IUPAC or Chemical Abstracts full name of the compound."

_chemical_conn_atom.charge.name: _chemical_conn_atom.charge
_chemical_conn_atom.charge.description:
The net integer charge assigned to this atom. This is the
               formal charge assignment normally found in chemical diagrams.

_chemical_conn_atom.display_x.name: _chemical_conn_atom.display_x
_chemical_conn_atom.display_x.description:
The 2D Cartesian x coordinate of the position of this atom in a
               recognizable chemical diagram. The coordinate origin is at the
               lower left corner, the x axis is horizontal and the y axis
               is vertical. The coordinates must lie in the range 0.0 to 1.0.
               These coordinates can be obtained from projections of a suitable
               uncluttered view of the molecular structure.

_chemical_conn_atom.display_y.name: _chemical_conn_atom.display_y
_chemical_conn_atom.display_y.description:
The 2D Cartesian y coordinate of the position of this atom in a
               recognizable chemical diagram. The coordinate origin is at the
               lower left corner, the x axis is horizontal and the y axis
               is vertical. The coordinates must lie in the range 0.0 to 1.0.
               These coordinates can be obtained from projections of a suitable
               uncluttered view of the molecular structure.

_chemical_conn_atom.NCA.name: _chemical_conn_atom.NCA
_chemical_conn_atom.NCA.description: "              The number of connected atoms excluding terminal hydrogen atoms."

_chemical_conn_atom.NH.name: _chemical_conn_atom.NH
_chemical_conn_atom.NH.description:
The total number of hydrogen atoms attached to this atom,
               regardless of whether they are included in the refinement or
               the ATOM_SITE list. This number is the same as
               _atom_site.attached_hydrogens only if none of the hydrogen
               atoms appear in the ATOM_SITE list.

_chemical_conn_atom.type_symbol.name: _chemical_conn_atom.type_symbol
_chemical_conn_atom.type_symbol.description:
This data item is a pointer to _atom_type.symbol in the
               ATOM_TYPE category.

_chemical_conn_bond.atom_1.name: _chemical_conn_bond.atom_1
_chemical_conn_bond.atom_1.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chemical_conn_atom.number in the
               CHEMICAL_CONN_ATOM category.

_chemical_conn_bond.atom_2.name: _chemical_conn_bond.atom_2
_chemical_conn_bond.atom_2.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chemical_conn_atom.number in the
               CHEMICAL_CONN_ATOM category.

_chemical_conn_bond.type.name: _chemical_conn_bond.type
_chemical_conn_bond.type.description:
The chemical bond type associated with the connection between
               the two sites _chemical_conn_bond.atom_1 and
               _chemical_conn_bond.atom_2.

_chemical_formula.analytical.name: _chemical_formula.analytical
_chemical_formula.analytical.description:
Formula determined by standard chemical analysis including trace
               elements. See the CHEMICAL_FORMULA category description for
               rules for writing chemical formulae. Parentheses are used only
               for standard uncertainties (estimated standard deviations).

_chemical_formula.entry_id.name: _chemical_formula.entry_id
_chemical_formula.entry_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category."

_chemical_formula.iupac.name: _chemical_formula.iupac
_chemical_formula.iupac.description:
Formula expressed in conformance with IUPAC rules for inorganic
               and metal-organic compounds where these conflict with the rules
               for any other CHEMICAL_FORMULA entries. Typically used for
               formatting a formula in accordance with journal rules. This
               should appear in the data block in addition to the most
               appropriate of the other CHEMICAL_FORMULA data names.

               Ref: IUPAC (1990). Nomenclature of Inorganic Chemistry.
                    Oxford: Blackwell Scientific Publications.
examples: "[Co Re (C12 H22 P)2 (C O)6].0.5C H3 O H"

_chemical_formula.moiety.name: _chemical_formula.moiety
_chemical_formula.moiety.description:
Formula with each discrete bonded residue or ion shown as a
               separate moiety. See the CHEMICAL_FORMULA category description
               for rules for writing chemical formulae. In addition to the
               general formulae requirements, the following rules apply:
                  (1) Moieties are separated by commas ','.
                  (2) The order of elements within a moiety follows general rule
                     (5) in the CHEMICAL_FORMULA category description.
                  (3) Parentheses are not used within moieties but may surround
                     a moiety. Parentheses may not be nested.
                  (4) Charges should be placed at the end of the moiety. The
                     charge '+' or '-' may be preceded by a numerical multiplier
                     and should be separated from the last (element symbol +
                     count) by a space. Pre- or post-multipliers may be used for
                     individual moieties.
examples: "C7 H4 Cl Hg N O3 S"

_chemical_formula.structural.name: _chemical_formula.structural
_chemical_formula.structural.description:
See the CHEMICAL_FORMULA category description for the rules for
               writing chemical formulae for inorganics, organometallics, metal
               complexes etc., in which bonded groups are preserved as
               discrete entities within parentheses, with post-multipliers as
               required. The order of the elements should give as much
               information as possible about the chemical structure.
               Parentheses may be used and nested as required. This formula
               should correspond to the structure as actually reported, i.e.
               trace elements not included in atom-type and atom-site data
               should not be included in this formula (see also
               _chemical_formula.analytical).

_chemical_formula.sum.name: _chemical_formula.sum
_chemical_formula.sum.description:
See the CHEMICAL_FORMULA category description for the rules
               for writing chemical formulae in which all discrete bonded
               residues and ions are summed over the constituent elements,
               following the ordering given in general rule (5) in the
               CHEMICAL_FORMULA category description. Parentheses are not
               normally used.

_chemical_formula.weight.name: _chemical_formula.weight
_chemical_formula.weight.description:
Formula mass in daltons. This mass should correspond to the
               formulae given under _chemical_formula.structural,
               _chemical_formula.moiety or _chemical_formula.sum and,
               together with the Z value and cell parameters, should
               yield the density given as _exptl_crystal.density_diffrn.

_chemical_formula.weight_meas.name: _chemical_formula.weight_meas
_chemical_formula.weight_meas.description: "              Formula mass in daltons measured by a non-diffraction experiment."

_citation.abstract.name: _citation.abstract
_citation.abstract.description:
Abstract for the citation. This is used most when the
               citation is extracted from a bibliographic database that
               contains full text or abstract information.

_citation.abstract_id_CAS.name: _citation.abstract_id_CAS
_citation.abstract_id_CAS.description:
The Chemical Abstracts Service (CAS) abstract identifier;
               relevant for journal articles.

_citation.book_id_ISBN.name: _citation.book_id_ISBN
_citation.book_id_ISBN.description:
The International Standard Book Number (ISBN) code assigned to
               the book cited; relevant for books or book chapters.

_citation.book_publisher.name: _citation.book_publisher
_citation.book_publisher.description:
The name of the publisher of the citation; relevant
               for books or book chapters.

_citation.book_publisher_city.name: _citation.book_publisher_city
_citation.book_publisher_city.description:
The location of the publisher of the citation; relevant
               for books or book chapters.

_citation.book_title.name: _citation.book_title
_citation.book_title.description:
The title of the book in which the citation appeared; relevant
               for books or book chapters.

_citation.coordinate_linkage.name: _citation.coordinate_linkage
_citation.coordinate_linkage.description:
_citation.coordinate_linkage states whether this citation
               is concerned with precisely the set of coordinates given in the
               data block. If, for instance, the publication described the same
               structure, but the coordinates had undergone further refinement
               prior to the creation of the data block, the value of this data
               item would be 'no'.

_citation.country.name: _citation.country
_citation.country.description:
The country/region of publication; relevant for books
               and book chapters.

_citation.database_id_Medline.name: _citation.database_id_Medline
_citation.database_id_Medline.description:
Accession number used by Medline to categorize a specific
               bibliographic entry.

_citation.details.name: _citation.details
_citation.details.description:
A description of special aspects of the relationship
               of the contents of the data block to the literature item cited.
examples:
citation relates to this precise
                                  coordinate set

citation relates to earlier low-resolution
                                  structure

citation relates to further refinement of
                                  structure reported in citation 2

_citation.journal_abbrev.name: _citation.journal_abbrev
_citation.journal_abbrev.description:
Abbreviated name of the cited journal as given in the
               Chemical Abstracts Service Source Index.

_citation.journal_id_ASTM.name: _citation.journal_id_ASTM
_citation.journal_id_ASTM.description:
The American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM) code
               assigned to the journal cited (also referred to as the CODEN
               designator of the Chemical Abstracts Service); relevant for
               journal articles.

_citation.journal_id_CSD.name: _citation.journal_id_CSD
_citation.journal_id_CSD.description:
The Cambridge Structural Database (CSD) code assigned to the
               journal cited; relevant for journal articles. This is also the
               system used at the Protein Data Bank (PDB).

_citation.journal_id_ISSN.name: _citation.journal_id_ISSN
_citation.journal_id_ISSN.description:
The International Standard Serial Number (ISSN) code assigned to
               the journal cited; relevant for journal articles.

_citation.journal_full.name: _citation.journal_full
_citation.journal_full.description: "              Full name of the cited journal; relevant for journal articles."

_citation.journal_issue.name: _citation.journal_issue
_citation.journal_issue.description:
Issue number of the journal cited; relevant for journal
               articles.

_citation.journal_volume.name: _citation.journal_volume
_citation.journal_volume.description:
Volume number of the journal cited; relevant for journal
               articles.

_citation.language.name: _citation.language
_citation.language.description: "              Language in which the cited article is written."

_citation.page_first.name: _citation.page_first
_citation.page_first.description:
The first page of the citation; relevant for journal
               articles, books and book chapters.

_citation.page_last.name: _citation.page_last
_citation.page_last.description:
The last page of the citation; relevant for journal
               articles, books and book chapters.

_citation.title.name: _citation.title
_citation.title.description:
The title of the citation; relevant for journal articles, books
               and book chapters.
examples:
Structure of diferric duck ovotransferrin
                                  at 2.35 Angstroms resolution.

_citation.year.name: _citation.year
_citation.year.description:
The year of the citation; relevant for journal articles, books
               and book chapters.

_citation_author.citation_id.name: _citation_author.citation_id
_citation_author.citation_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _citation.id in the CITATION
               category.

_citation_author.name.name: _citation_author.name
_citation_author.name.description:
Name of an author of the citation; relevant for journal
               articles, books and book chapters.

               The family name(s), followed by a comma and including any
               dynastic components, precedes the first name(s) or initial(s).

_citation_author.ordinal.name: _citation_author.ordinal
_citation_author.ordinal.description:
This data item defines the order of the author's name in the
               list of authors of a citation.

_citation_author.identifier_ORCID.name: _citation_author.identifier_ORCID
_citation_author.identifier_ORCID.description: "    The Open Researcher and Contributor ID (ORCID)."

_citation_editor.citation_id.name: _citation_editor.citation_id
_citation_editor.citation_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _citation.id in the CITATION
               category.

_citation_editor.name.name: _citation_editor.name
_citation_editor.name.description:
Names of an editor of the citation; relevant for books and
               book chapters.

               The family name(s), followed by a comma and including any
               dynastic components, precedes the first name(s) or initial(s).

_citation_editor.ordinal.name: _citation_editor.ordinal
_citation_editor.ordinal.description:
This data item defines the order of the editor's name in the
               list of editors of a citation.

_computing.entry_id.name: _computing.entry_id
_computing.entry_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category."

_computing.cell_refinement.name: _computing.cell_refinement
_computing.cell_refinement.description:
Software used for cell refinement.

               Give the program or package name and a brief reference.

_computing.data_collection.name: _computing.data_collection
_computing.data_collection.description:
Software used for data collection.

               Give the program or package name and a brief reference.

_computing.data_reduction.name: _computing.data_reduction
_computing.data_reduction.description:
Software used for data reduction.

               Give the program or package name and a brief reference.

_computing.molecular_graphics.name: _computing.molecular_graphics
_computing.molecular_graphics.description:
Software used for molecular graphics.

               Give the program or package name and a brief reference.

_computing.publication_material.name: _computing.publication_material
_computing.publication_material.description:
Software used for generating material for publication.

               Give the program or package name and a brief reference.

_computing.structure_refinement.name: _computing.structure_refinement
_computing.structure_refinement.description:
Software used for refinement of the structure.

               Give the program or package name and a brief reference.

_computing.structure_solution.name: _computing.structure_solution
_computing.structure_solution.description:
Software used for solution of the structure.

               Give the program or package name and a brief reference.

_database.entry_id.name: _database.entry_id
_database.entry_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category."

_database.journal_ASTM.description:

_database.journal_CSD.name: _database.journal_CSD
_database.journal_CSD.description: "              The journal code used in the Cambridge Structural Database."

_database_2.database_id.description: "              An abbreviation that identifies the database."

_database_2.database_code.description:

_database_PDB_matrix.scale_vector[1].description: "              The [1] element of the PDB SCALE vector."

_database_PDB_matrix.scale_vector[2].description: "              The [2] element of the PDB SCALE vector."

_database_PDB_matrix.scale_vector[3].description: "              The [3] element of the PDB SCALE vector."

_database_PDB_remark.id.name: _database_PDB_remark.id
_database_PDB_remark.id.description: "              A unique identifier for the PDB remark record."

_database_PDB_remark.text.name: _database_PDB_remark.text
_database_PDB_remark.text.description: "              The full text of the PDB remark record."

_database_PDB_rev.author_name.name: _database_PDB_rev.author_name
_database_PDB_rev.author_name.description:
The name of the person responsible for submitting this revision
               to the PDB.

               The family name(s) followed by a comma precedes the first
               name(s) or initial(s).

_database_PDB_rev.date.name: _database_PDB_rev.date
_database_PDB_rev.date.description: "              Date the PDB revision took place. Taken from the REVDAT record."

_database_PDB_rev.date_original.name: _database_PDB_rev.date_original
_database_PDB_rev.date_original.description:
Date the entry first entered the PDB database in the form
               yyyy-mm-dd. Taken from the PDB HEADER record.

_database_PDB_rev.mod_type.name: _database_PDB_rev.mod_type
_database_PDB_rev.mod_type.description:
A code taken from the REVDAT record classifying common types of entry
               revisions.

enumeration.detail:
modifications affecting the coordinates
                                      or their transforms (CRYST1, ORIGX, SCALE,
                                      MTRIX, TVECT, ATOM, HETATM, SIGATM
                                      records)

layer 1 to layer 2 revision which may affect
                                   all record types

_database_PDB_rev.replaced_by.name: _database_PDB_rev.replaced_by
_database_PDB_rev.replaced_by.description:
The PDB code for a subsequent PDB entry that replaced the
               PDB file corresponding to this data block.

_database_PDB_rev.replaces.name: _database_PDB_rev.replaces
_database_PDB_rev.replaces.description:
The PDB code for a previous PDB entry that was replaced by
               the PDB file corresponding to this data block.

_database_PDB_rev_record.details.name: _database_PDB_rev_record.details
_database_PDB_rev_record.details.description:

               this PDB entry.

_database_PDB_rev_record.rev_num.name: _database_PDB_rev_record.rev_num
_database_PDB_rev_record.rev_num.description:
This data item is a pointer to _database_PDB_rev.num in the
               DATABASE_PDB_REV category.

_database_PDB_rev_record.type.name: _database_PDB_rev_record.type
_database_PDB_rev_record.type.description:
The types of records that were changed in this revision to a
               PDB entry.

_database_PDB_tvect.details.name: _database_PDB_tvect.details
_database_PDB_tvect.details.description: "              A description of special aspects of this TVECT."

_database_PDB_tvect.id.name: _database_PDB_tvect.id
_database_PDB_tvect.id.description:
The value of _database_PDB_tvect.id must uniquely identify a
               record in the DATABASE_PDB_TVECT list.

               Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique
               identifier.

_database_PDB_tvect.vector[1].name: _database_PDB_tvect.vector[1]
_database_PDB_tvect.vector[1].description: "              The [1] element of the PDB TVECT vector."

_database_PDB_tvect.vector[2].name: _database_PDB_tvect.vector[2]
_database_PDB_tvect.vector[2].description: "              The [2] element of the PDB TVECT vector."

_database_PDB_tvect.vector[3].name: _database_PDB_tvect.vector[3]
_database_PDB_tvect.vector[3].description: "              The [3] element of the PDB TVECT vector."

_diffrn.ambient_environment.name: _diffrn.ambient_environment
_diffrn.ambient_environment.description: "              The gas or liquid surrounding the sample, if not air."

_diffrn.ambient_temp.name: _diffrn.ambient_temp
_diffrn.ambient_temp.description:
The mean temperature in kelvins at which the intensities were
               measured.

_diffrn.ambient_temp_details.name: _diffrn.ambient_temp_details
_diffrn.ambient_temp_details.description:
A description of special aspects of temperature control during
               data collection.

_diffrn.ambient_temp_esd.name: _diffrn.ambient_temp_esd
_diffrn.ambient_temp_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _diffrn.ambient_temp.

_diffrn.crystal_id.name: _diffrn.crystal_id
_diffrn.crystal_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _exptl_crystal.id in the
               EXPTL_CRYSTAL category.

_diffrn.crystal_support.name: _diffrn.crystal_support
_diffrn.crystal_support.description:
The physical device used to support the crystal during data
               collection.

_diffrn.crystal_treatment.name: _diffrn.crystal_treatment
_diffrn.crystal_treatment.description:
Remarks about how the crystal was treated prior to intensity
               measurement. Particularly relevant when intensities were
               measured at low temperature.

_diffrn.details.name: _diffrn.details
_diffrn.details.description:
Special details of the diffraction measurement process. Should
               include information about source instability, crystal motion,
               degradation and so on.

_diffrn_attenuator.code.name: _diffrn_attenuator.code
_diffrn_attenuator.code.description:
A code associated with a particular attenuator setting. This
               code is referenced by the _diffrn_refln.attenuator_code which is
               stored with the diffraction data. See _diffrn_attenuator.scale.

_diffrn_attenuator.scale.name: _diffrn_attenuator.scale
_diffrn_attenuator.scale.description:
The scale factor applied when an intensity measurement is
               reduced by an attenuator identified by _diffrn_attenuator.code.
               The measured intensity must be multiplied by this scale to
                convert it to the same scale as unattenuated intensities.

_diffrn_detector.details.name: _diffrn_detector.details
_diffrn_detector.details.description: "              A description of special aspects of the radiation detector."

_diffrn_detector.detector.name: _diffrn_detector.detector
_diffrn_detector.detector.description: "              The general class of the radiation detector."

_diffrn_detector.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_detector.diffrn_id
_diffrn_detector.diffrn_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN
               category.

_diffrn_detector.type.name: _diffrn_detector.type
_diffrn_detector.type.description: "              The make, model or name of the detector device used."

_diffrn_measurement.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_measurement.diffrn_id
_diffrn_measurement.diffrn_id.description:

               category.

_diffrn_measurement.details.description: "              A description of special aspects of the intensity measurement."
