440 frames, 0.20 degrees, 150 sec, detector
                                  distance 12 cm, detector angle 22.5 degrees

_diffrn_measurement.device.name: _diffrn_measurement.device
_diffrn_measurement.device.description:
The general class of  goniometer or device used to support and
               orient the specimen.

_diffrn_measurement.device_details.name: _diffrn_measurement.device_details
_diffrn_measurement.device_details.description:
A description of special aspects of the device used to measure
               the diffraction intensities.
examples:
commercial goniometer modified locally to
                                 allow for 90\% \t arc

_diffrn_measurement.device_type.name: _diffrn_measurement.device_type
_diffrn_measurement.device_type.description:
The make, model or name of the measurement device
               (goniometer) used.

_diffrn_measurement.specimen_support.name: _diffrn_measurement.specimen_support
_diffrn_measurement.specimen_support.description:
The physical device used to support the crystal during data
               collection.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.diffrn_id
_diffrn_orient_matrix.diffrn_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN
               category.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.type.name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.type
_diffrn_orient_matrix.type.description:
A description of the orientation matrix type and how it should
               be applied to define the orientation of the crystal precisely
               with respect to the diffractometer axes.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][1].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][1]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][1].description:
The [1][1] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions
               of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the
               local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][2].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][2]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][2].description:
The [1][2] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions
               of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the
               local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][3].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][3]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[1][3].description:
The [1][3] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions
               of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the
               local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][1].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][1]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][1].description:
The [2][1] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions
               of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the
               local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][2].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][2]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][2].description:
The [2][2] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions
               of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the
               local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][3].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][3]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[2][3].description:
The [2][3] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions
               of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the
               local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][1].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][1]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][1].description:
The [3][1] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions
               of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the
               local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][2].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][2]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][2].description:
The [3][2] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions
               of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the
               local diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][3].name: _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][3]
_diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[3][3].description:
The [3][3] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the dimensions
               of the reciprocal cell and its orientation with respect to the
               local  diffractometer axes. See also _diffrn_orient_matrix.type.

_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_chi.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.angle_chi
_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_chi.description:
Diffractometer angle chi of a reflection used to
               define the orientation matrix in degrees. See
               _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[][] and the Miller indices
               in the DIFFRN_ORIENT_REFLN category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_kappa.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.angle_kappa
_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_kappa.description:
Diffractometer angle kappa of a reflection used to
               define the orientation matrix in degrees. See
               _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[][] and the Miller indices
               in the DIFFRN_ORIENT_REFLN category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_omega.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.angle_omega
_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_omega.description:
Diffractometer angle omega of a reflection used to
               define the orientation matrix in degrees. See
               _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[][] and the Miller indices in
               the DIFFRN_ORIENT_REFLN category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_phi.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.angle_phi
_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_phi.description:
Diffractometer angle phi of a reflection used to
               define the orientation matrix in degrees. See
               _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[][] and the Miller indices
               in the DIFFRN_ORIENT_REFLN category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_psi.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.angle_psi
_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_psi.description:
Diffractometer angle psi of a reflection used to
               define the orientation matrix in degrees. See
               _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[][] and the Miller indices
               in the DIFFRN_ORIENT_REFLN category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_theta.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.angle_theta
_diffrn_orient_refln.angle_theta.description:
Diffractometer angle theta of a reflection used to
               define the orientation matrix in degrees. See
               _diffrn_orient_matrix.UB[][] and the Miller indices
               in the DIFFRN_ORIENT_REFLN category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.diffrn_id
_diffrn_orient_refln.diffrn_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN
               category.

_diffrn_orient_refln.index_h.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.index_h
_diffrn_orient_refln.index_h.description:
Miller index h of a reflection used to define the orientation
               matrix.

_diffrn_orient_refln.index_k.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.index_k
_diffrn_orient_refln.index_k.description:
Miller index k of a reflection used to define the orientation
               matrix.

_diffrn_orient_refln.index_l.name: _diffrn_orient_refln.index_l
_diffrn_orient_refln.index_l.description:
Miller index l of a reflection used to define the orientation
               matrix.

_diffrn_radiation.collimation.name: _diffrn_radiation.collimation
_diffrn_radiation.collimation.description: "              The collimation or focusing applied to the radiation."

_diffrn_radiation.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_radiation.diffrn_id
_diffrn_radiation.diffrn_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN
               category.

_diffrn_radiation.filter_edge.name: _diffrn_radiation.filter_edge
_diffrn_radiation.filter_edge.description: "              Absorption edge in angstroms of the radiation filter used."

_diffrn_radiation.inhomogeneity.name: _diffrn_radiation.inhomogeneity
_diffrn_radiation.inhomogeneity.description:
Half-width in millimetres of the incident beam in the
               direction perpendicular to the diffraction plane.

_diffrn_radiation.monochromator.name: _diffrn_radiation.monochromator
_diffrn_radiation.monochromator.description:
The method used to obtain monochromatic radiation. If a mono-
               chromator crystal is used, the material and the indices of the
               Bragg reflection are specified.

_diffrn_radiation.polarisn_norm.name: _diffrn_radiation.polarisn_norm
_diffrn_radiation.polarisn_norm.description:
The angle in degrees, as viewed from the specimen, between the
               perpendicular component of the polarization and the diffraction
               plane. See _diffrn_radiation.polarisn_ratio.

_diffrn_radiation.polarisn_ratio.name: _diffrn_radiation.polarisn_ratio
_diffrn_radiation.polarisn_ratio.description:
Polarization ratio of the diffraction beam incident on the
               crystal. This is the ratio of the perpendicularly polarized
               to the parallel-polarized component of the radiation. The
               perpendicular component forms an angle of
               _diffrn_radiation.polarisn_norm to the normal to the
               diffraction plane of the sample (i.e. the plane containing
               the incident and reflected beams).

_diffrn_radiation.probe.name: _diffrn_radiation.probe
_diffrn_radiation.probe.description:
The nature of the radiation used (i.e. the name of the
              subatomic particle or the region of the electromagnetic
              spectrum). It is strongly recommended that this information
              is given, so that the probe radiation can be simply determined.

_diffrn_radiation.type.name: _diffrn_radiation.type
_diffrn_radiation.type.description:
The nature of the radiation. This is typically a description
               of the X-ray wavelength in Siegbahn notation.

_diffrn_radiation.xray_symbol.name: _diffrn_radiation.xray_symbol
_diffrn_radiation.xray_symbol.description:
The IUPAC symbol for the X-ray wavelength for the probe
               radiation.

enumeration.detail: "use where K-L~3~ and K-L~2~ are not resolved"

_diffrn_radiation.wavelength_id.name: _diffrn_radiation.wavelength_id
_diffrn_radiation.wavelength_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _diffrn_radiation_wavelength.id
               in the DIFFRN_RADIATION_WAVELENGTH category.

_diffrn_radiation_wavelength.wt.name: _diffrn_radiation_wavelength.wt
_diffrn_radiation_wavelength.wt.description:
The relative weight of a wavelength identified by the code
               _diffrn_radiation_wavelength.id in the list of wavelengths.

_diffrn_refln.angle_chi.name: _diffrn_refln.angle_chi
_diffrn_refln.angle_chi.description:
The diffractometer angle chi of a reflection in degrees. This
               angle corresponds to the specified orientation matrix
               and the original measured cell before any subsequent cell
               transformations.

_diffrn_refln.angle_kappa.name: _diffrn_refln.angle_kappa
_diffrn_refln.angle_kappa.description:
The diffractometer angle kappa of a reflection in degrees. This
               angle corresponds to the specified orientation matrix
               and the original measured cell before any subsequent cell
               transformations.

_diffrn_refln.angle_omega.name: _diffrn_refln.angle_omega
_diffrn_refln.angle_omega.description:
The diffractometer angle omega of a reflection in degrees. This
               angle corresponds to the specified orientation matrix
               and the original measured cell before any subsequent cell
               transformations.

_diffrn_refln.angle_phi.name: _diffrn_refln.angle_phi
_diffrn_refln.angle_phi.description:
The diffractometer angle phi of a reflection in degrees. This
               angle corresponds to the specified orientation matrix
               and the original measured cell before any subsequent cell
               transformations.

_diffrn_refln.angle_psi.name: _diffrn_refln.angle_psi
_diffrn_refln.angle_psi.description:
The diffractometer angle psi of a reflection in degrees. This
               angle corresponds  to the specified orientation matrix
               and the original measured cell before any subsequent cell
               transformations.

_diffrn_refln.angle_theta.name: _diffrn_refln.angle_theta
_diffrn_refln.angle_theta.description:
The diffractometer angle theta of a reflection in degrees. This
               angle corresponds  to the specified orientation matrix
               and the original measured cell before any subsequent cell
               transformations.

_diffrn_refln.attenuator_code.name: _diffrn_refln.attenuator_code
_diffrn_refln.attenuator_code.description:
The code identifying the attenuator setting for this reflection.
               This code must match one of the _diffrn_attenuator.code values.

_diffrn_refln.counts_bg_1.name: _diffrn_refln.counts_bg_1
_diffrn_refln.counts_bg_1.description:
The diffractometer counts for the measurement of the background
               before the peak.

_diffrn_refln.counts_bg_2.name: _diffrn_refln.counts_bg_2
_diffrn_refln.counts_bg_2.description:
The diffractometer counts for the measurement of the background
               after the peak.

_diffrn_refln.counts_net.name: _diffrn_refln.counts_net
_diffrn_refln.counts_net.description:
The diffractometer counts for the measurement of net counts after
               background removal.

_diffrn_refln.counts_peak.name: _diffrn_refln.counts_peak
_diffrn_refln.counts_peak.description:
The diffractometer counts for the measurement of counts for the
               peak scan or position.

_diffrn_refln.counts_total.name: _diffrn_refln.counts_total
_diffrn_refln.counts_total.description:
The diffractometer counts for the measurement of total counts
               (background plus peak).

_diffrn_refln.detect_slit_horiz.name: _diffrn_refln.detect_slit_horiz
_diffrn_refln.detect_slit_horiz.description: "              Total slit aperture in degrees in the diffraction plane."

_diffrn_refln.detect_slit_vert.name: _diffrn_refln.detect_slit_vert
_diffrn_refln.detect_slit_vert.description:
Total slit aperture in degrees perpendicular to the
               diffraction plane.

_diffrn_refln.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_refln.diffrn_id
_diffrn_refln.diffrn_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN
               category.

_diffrn_refln.elapsed_time.name: _diffrn_refln.elapsed_time
_diffrn_refln.elapsed_time.description:
Elapsed time in minutes from the start of the diffraction
               experiment to the measurement of this intensity.

_diffrn_refln.id.name: _diffrn_refln.id
_diffrn_refln.id.description:
The value of _diffrn_refln.id must uniquely identify the
               reflection in the data set identified by the item
              _diffrn_refln.diffrn_id.

               Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique
               identifier.

_diffrn_refln.index_h.name: _diffrn_refln.index_h
_diffrn_refln.index_h.description:
Miller index h of a reflection. The values of
               the Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category need not match
               the values of the Miller indices in the REFLN category if a
               transformation of the original measured cell has taken place.
               Details of the cell transformation are given in
               _diffrn_reflns.reduction_process. See also
               _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[][].

_diffrn_refln.index_k.name: _diffrn_refln.index_k
_diffrn_refln.index_k.description:
Miller index k of a reflection. The values of
               the Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category need not match
               the values of the Miller indices in the REFLN category if a
               transformation of the original measured cell has taken place.
               Details of the cell transformation are given in
               _diffrn_reflns.reduction_process. See also
               _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[][].

_diffrn_refln.index_l.name: _diffrn_refln.index_l
_diffrn_refln.index_l.description:
Miller index l of a reflection. The values of
               the Miller indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category need not match
               the values of the Miller indices in the REFLN category if a
               transformation of the original measured cell has taken place.
               Details of the cell transformation are given in
               _diffrn_reflns.reduction_process. See also
               _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[][].

_diffrn_refln.intensity_net.name: _diffrn_refln.intensity_net
_diffrn_refln.intensity_net.description:
Net intensity calculated from the diffraction counts after the
               attenuator and standard scales have been applied.

_diffrn_refln.intensity_sigma.name: _diffrn_refln.intensity_sigma
_diffrn_refln.intensity_sigma.description:
Standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of the
               intensity calculated from the diffraction counts after the
               attenuator and standard scales have been applied.

_diffrn_refln.scale_group_code.name: _diffrn_refln.scale_group_code
_diffrn_refln.scale_group_code.description:
The code identifying the scale applying to this reflection.

               This data item is a pointer to _diffrn_scale_group.code in the
               DIFFRN_SCALE_GROUP category.

_diffrn_refln.scan_mode.name: _diffrn_refln.scan_mode
_diffrn_refln.scan_mode.description:
The code identifying the mode of scanning for measurements
               using a diffractometer.
               See _diffrn_refln.scan_width and _diffrn_refln.scan_mode_backgd.

_diffrn_refln.scan_mode_backgd.name: _diffrn_refln.scan_mode_backgd
_diffrn_refln.scan_mode_backgd.description:
The code identifying the mode of scanning a reflection to
               measure the background intensity.

_diffrn_refln.scan_rate.name: _diffrn_refln.scan_rate
_diffrn_refln.scan_rate.description:
The rate of scanning a reflection in degrees per minute
               to measure the intensity.

_diffrn_refln.scan_time_backgd.name: _diffrn_refln.scan_time_backgd
_diffrn_refln.scan_time_backgd.description: "              The time spent measuring each background in seconds."

_diffrn_refln.scan_width.name: _diffrn_refln.scan_width
_diffrn_refln.scan_width.description:
The scan width in degrees of the scan mode defined by the code
               _diffrn_refln.scan_mode.

_diffrn_refln.sint_over_lambda.name: _diffrn_refln.sint_over_lambda
_diffrn_refln.sint_over_lambda.description:
The (sin theta)/lambda value in reciprocal angstroms for this
               reflection.

_diffrn_refln.standard_code.name: _diffrn_refln.standard_code
_diffrn_refln.standard_code.description:
The code identifying that this reflection was measured as a
               standard intensity.

               This data item is a pointer to _diffrn_standard_refln.code in the
               DIFFRN_STANDARD_REFLN category.

_diffrn_refln.wavelength.name: _diffrn_refln.wavelength
_diffrn_refln.wavelength.description:
The mean wavelength in angstroms of the radiation used to measure
               the intensity of this reflection. This is an important parameter
               for data collected using energy-dispersive detectors or the
               Laue method.

_diffrn_refln.wavelength_id.name: _diffrn_refln.wavelength_id
_diffrn_refln.wavelength_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _diffrn_radiation.wavelength_id in
               the DIFFRN_RADIATION category.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_image_id.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_image_id
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_image_id.description:
The identifier of the diffraction image this reflection
            is associated with. This is often the image where the
            calculated reflection centroid is predicted to occur.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_scan_angle.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_scan_angle
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_scan_angle.description:
The value of the angle around the scan axis for this
               reflection. This is often the scan angle at which the
               calculated reflection centroid is predicted to occur.

_diffrn_reflns.av_R_equivalents.name: _diffrn_reflns.av_R_equivalents
_diffrn_reflns.av_R_equivalents.description:
The residual [sum|avdel(I)| / sum|av(I)|] for symmetry-equivalent
               reflections used to calculate the average intensity av(I). The
               avdel(I) term is the average absolute difference between av(I)
               and the individual symmetry-equivalent intensities.

_diffrn_reflns.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_reflns.diffrn_id
_diffrn_reflns.diffrn_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN
               category.

_diffrn_reflns.limit_h_max.name: _diffrn_reflns.limit_h_max
_diffrn_reflns.limit_h_max.description:
The maximum value of the Miller index h for the
               reflection data specified by _diffrn_refln.index_h.

_diffrn_reflns.limit_h_min.name: _diffrn_reflns.limit_h_min
_diffrn_reflns.limit_h_min.description:
The minimum value of the Miller index h for the
               reflection data specified by _diffrn_refln.index_h.

_diffrn_reflns.limit_k_max.name: _diffrn_reflns.limit_k_max
_diffrn_reflns.limit_k_max.description:
The maximum value of the Miller index k for the
               reflection data specified by _diffrn_refln.index_k.

_diffrn_reflns.limit_k_min.name: _diffrn_reflns.limit_k_min
_diffrn_reflns.limit_k_min.description:
The minimum value of the Miller index k for the
               reflection data specified by _diffrn_refln.index_k.

_diffrn_reflns.limit_l_max.name: _diffrn_reflns.limit_l_max
_diffrn_reflns.limit_l_max.description:
The maximum value of the Miller index l for the
               reflection data specified by _diffrn_refln.index_l.

_diffrn_reflns.limit_l_min.name: _diffrn_reflns.limit_l_min
_diffrn_reflns.limit_l_min.description:
The minimum value of the Miller index l for the
               reflection data specified by _diffrn_refln.index_l.

_diffrn_reflns.number.name: _diffrn_reflns.number
_diffrn_reflns.number.description:
The total number of measured intensities, excluding reflections
               that are classified as systematically absent.

_diffrn_reflns.reduction_process.name: _diffrn_reflns.reduction_process
_diffrn_reflns.reduction_process.description:
A description of the process used to reduce the intensity data
               into structure-factor magnitudes.

_diffrn_reflns.theta_max.name: _diffrn_reflns.theta_max
_diffrn_reflns.theta_max.description:
Maximum theta angle in degrees for the measured diffraction
               intensities.

_diffrn_reflns.theta_min.name: _diffrn_reflns.theta_min
_diffrn_reflns.theta_min.description:
Minimum theta angle in degrees for the measured diffraction
               intensities.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][1].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][1]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][1].description:
The [1][1] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller
               indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in
               the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][2].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][2]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][2].description:
The [1][2] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller
               indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in
               the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][3].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][3]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[1][3].description:
The [1][3] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller
               indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in
               the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][1].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][1]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][1].description:
The [2][1] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller
               indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in
               the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][2].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][2]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][2].description:
The [2][2] element of the 3x3 matrix used to transform Miller
               indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in
               the REFLN category.

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][3].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][3]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[2][3].description:

               indices in the DIFFRN_REFLN category into the Miller indices in

_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[3][3].name: _diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[3][3]
_diffrn_reflns.transf_matrix[3][3].description:

_diffrn_scale_group.code.name: _diffrn_scale_group.code
_diffrn_scale_group.code.description:
The value of _diffrn_scale_group.code must uniquely identify a
               record in the DIFFRN_SCALE_GROUP list.

               Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique
               identifier.

_diffrn_scale_group.I_net.name: _diffrn_scale_group.I_net
_diffrn_scale_group.I_net.description:
The scale for a specific measurement group which is to be
               multiplied with the net intensity to place all intensities
               in the DIFFRN_REFLN or REFLN list on a common scale.

_diffrn_source.current.name: _diffrn_source.current
_diffrn_source.current.description:
The current in milliamperes at which the radiation source
               was operated.

_diffrn_source.details.name: _diffrn_source.details
_diffrn_source.details.description: "              A description of special aspects of the radiation source used."

_diffrn_source.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_source.diffrn_id
_diffrn_source.diffrn_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN
               category.

_diffrn_source.power.name: _diffrn_source.power
_diffrn_source.power.description:
The power in kilowatts at which the radiation source
               was operated.

_diffrn_source.size.name: _diffrn_source.size
_diffrn_source.size.description: "              The dimensions of the source as viewed from the sample."

_diffrn_source.source.name: _diffrn_source.source
_diffrn_source.source.description: "              The general class of the radiation source."

_diffrn_source.target.name: _diffrn_source.target
_diffrn_source.target.description:
The chemical element symbol for the X-ray target
               (usually the anode) used to generate X-rays.
               This can also be used for spallation sources.

_diffrn_source.type.name: _diffrn_source.type
_diffrn_source.type.description: "              The make, model or name of the source of radiation."

_diffrn_source.voltage.name: _diffrn_source.voltage
_diffrn_source.voltage.description:
The voltage in kilovolts at which the radiation source was
               operated.

_diffrn_standard_refln.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_standard_refln.diffrn_id
_diffrn_standard_refln.diffrn_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN
               category.

_diffrn_standard_refln.index_h.name: _diffrn_standard_refln.index_h
_diffrn_standard_refln.index_h.description:
Miller index h of a standard reflection used in the diffraction
               measurement process.

_diffrn_standard_refln.index_k.name: _diffrn_standard_refln.index_k
_diffrn_standard_refln.index_k.description:
Miller index k of a standard reflection used in the diffraction
               measurement process.

_diffrn_standard_refln.index_l.name: _diffrn_standard_refln.index_l
_diffrn_standard_refln.index_l.description:
Miller index l of a standard reflection used in the diffraction
               measurement process.

_diffrn_standards.diffrn_id.name: _diffrn_standards.diffrn_id
_diffrn_standards.diffrn_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _diffrn.id in the DIFFRN
               category.

_diffrn_standards.decay_%.name: _diffrn_standards.decay_%
_diffrn_standards.decay_%.description:
The percentage decrease in the mean of the intensities
               for the set of standard reflections from the start of the
               measurement process to the end. This value usually
               affords a measure of the overall decay in crystal quality
               during the diffraction measurement process. Negative values
               are used in exceptional instances where the final intensities
               are greater than the initial ones.

_diffrn_standards.interval_count.name: _diffrn_standards.interval_count
_diffrn_standards.interval_count.description:
The number of reflection intensities between the measurement of
               standard reflection intensities.

_diffrn_standards.interval_time.name: _diffrn_standards.interval_time
_diffrn_standards.interval_time.description:
The time in minutes between the measurement of standard
               reflection intensities.

_diffrn_standards.number.name: _diffrn_standards.number
_diffrn_standards.number.description:
The number of unique standard reflections used during the
               measurement of  the diffraction intensities.

_diffrn_standards.scale_sigma.name: _diffrn_standards.scale_sigma
_diffrn_standards.scale_sigma.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of
               the individual mean standard scales applied to the intensity
               data.

_entity.details.name: _entity.details
_entity.details.description: "              A description of special aspects of the entity."

_entity.formula_weight.name: _entity.formula_weight
_entity.formula_weight.description: "              Formula mass in daltons of the entity."

_entity.src_method.name: _entity.src_method
_entity.src_method.description:
The method by which the sample for the entity was produced.
               Entities isolated directly from natural sources (tissues, soil
               samples etc.) are expected to have further information in the
               ENTITY_SRC_NAT category. Entities isolated from genetically
               manipulated sources are expected to have further information in
               the ENTITY_SRC_GEN category.

enumeration.detail: "                                 entity isolated from a natural source"

entity isolated from a genetically
                                  manipulated source

_entity.type.name: _entity.type
_entity.type.description:
Defines the type of the entity.

               Polymer entities are expected to have corresponding
               ENTITY_POLY and associated entries.

               Non-polymer entities are expected to have corresponding
               CHEM_COMP and associated entries.

               Water entities are not expected to have corresponding
               entries in the ENTITY category.

_entity_keywords.entity_id.name: _entity_keywords.entity_id
_entity_keywords.entity_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entity.id in the ENTITY category."

_entity_link.link_id.name: _entity_link.link_id
_entity_link.link_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _chem_link.id in the
               CHEM_LINK category.

_entity_link.details.name: _entity_link.details
_entity_link.details.description:
A description of special aspects of a link between
               chemical components in the structure.

_entity_link.entity_id_1.name: _entity_link.entity_id_1
_entity_link.entity_id_1.description:
The entity ID of the first of the two entities joined by the
               link.

               This data item is a pointer to _entity.id in the ENTITY
               category.

_entity_link.entity_id_2.name: _entity_link.entity_id_2
_entity_link.entity_id_2.description:

_entity_link.entity_seq_num_1.description:
For a polymer entity, the sequence number in the first of
               the two entities containing the link.

               This data item is a pointer to _entity_poly_seq.num in the
               ENTITY_POLY_SEQ category.

_entity_link.entity_seq_num_2.name: _entity_link.entity_seq_num_2
_entity_link.entity_seq_num_2.description:

               the two entities containing the link.

               This data item is a pointer to _entity_poly_seq.num in the
               ENTITY_POLY_SEQ category.

_entity_poly.entity_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entity.id in the ENTITY category."

_entry_link.entry_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category."

_entry_link.id.description:
The value of _entry_link.id identifies a data block

_exptl.absorpt_coefficient_mu.name: _exptl.absorpt_coefficient_mu
_exptl.absorpt_coefficient_mu.description:
The absorption coefficient mu in reciprocal millimetres
               calculated from the atomic content of the cell, the density and
               the radiation wavelength.

_exptl.absorpt_correction_T_max.name: _exptl.absorpt_correction_T_max
_exptl.absorpt_correction_T_max.description:
The maximum transmission factor for the crystal and radiation.
               The maximum and minimum transmission factors are also referred

               A or 1/A*.

_exptl.absorpt_correction_T_min.name: _exptl.absorpt_correction_T_min
_exptl.absorpt_correction_T_min.description:
The minimum transmission factor for the crystal and radiation.
               The maximum and minimum transmission factors are also referred
               to as the absorption correction
               A or 1/A*.

_exptl.absorpt_correction_type.name: _exptl.absorpt_correction_type
_exptl.absorpt_correction_type.description:
The absorption correction type and method. The value
               'empirical' should NOT be used unless more detailed
               information is not available.

_exptl.absorpt_process_details.name: _exptl.absorpt_process_details
_exptl.absorpt_process_details.description:
Description of the absorption process applied to the
               intensities. A literature reference should be supplied for
               psi-scan techniques.

_exptl.entry_id.name: _exptl.entry_id
_exptl.entry_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category."

_exptl.crystals_number.name: _exptl.crystals_number
_exptl.crystals_number.description:
The total number of crystals used in the  measurement of

_exptl.details.name: _exptl.details
_exptl.details.description:
Any special information about the experimental work prior to the
               intensity measurement. See also _exptl_crystal.preparation.

_exptl.method.description: "              The method used in the experiment."

enumeration.detail: "IR and FTIR only as supporting methods"
"FLUORESCENCE TRANSFER only as a supporting method"
"THEORETICAL MODEL only as a supporting method"

_exptl.method_details.name: _exptl.method_details
_exptl.method_details.description: "              A description of special aspects of the experimental method."

_exptl_crystal.density_diffrn.name: _exptl_crystal.density_diffrn
_exptl_crystal.density_diffrn.description:
Density values calculated from the crystal cell and contents. The
               units are megagrams per cubic metre (grams per cubic centimetre).

_exptl_crystal.density_Matthews.name: _exptl_crystal.density_Matthews
_exptl_crystal.density_Matthews.description:
The density of the crystal, expressed as the ratio of the
               volume of the asymmetric unit to the molecular mass of a
               monomer of the structure, in units of angstroms^3^ per dalton.

               Ref: Matthews, B. W. (1968). J. Mol. Biol. 33, 491-497.

_exptl_crystal.density_percent_sol.name: _exptl_crystal.density_percent_sol
_exptl_crystal.density_percent_sol.description:
Density value P calculated from the crystal cell and contents,
               expressed as per cent solvent.

               P = 1 - (1.23 N MMass) / V

               N     = the number of molecules in the unit cell
               MMass = the molecular mass of each molecule (gm/mole)
               V     = the volume of the unit cell (A^3^)
               1.23  = a conversion factor evaluated as:

                       (0.74 cm^3^/g) (10^24^ A^3^/cm^3^)
                       --------------------------------------
                            (6.02*10^23^) molecules/mole

                       where 0.74 is an assumed value for the partial specific
                       volume of the molecule

_exptl_crystal.description.name: _exptl_crystal.description
_exptl_crystal.description.description:
A description of the quality and habit of the crystal.
               The crystal dimensions should not normally be reported here;
               use instead the specific items in the EXPTL_CRYSTAL category
               relating to size for the gross dimensions of the crystal and
               data items in the EXPTL_CRYSTAL_FACE category to describe the
               relationship between individual faces.

_exptl_crystal.F_000.name: _exptl_crystal.F_000
_exptl_crystal.F_000.description:
The effective number of electrons in the crystal unit cell
               contributing to F(000). This may contain dispersion contributions
               and is calculated as

               F(000) = [ sum (f~r~^2^ + f~i~^2^) ]^1/2^

               f~r~ = real part of the scattering factors at theta = 0 degree
               f~i~ = imaginary part of the scattering factors at
               theta = 0 degree

                      the sum is taken over each atom in the unit cell

_exptl_crystal.preparation.name: _exptl_crystal.preparation
_exptl_crystal.preparation.description:
Details of crystal growth and preparation of the crystal (e.g.
               mounting) prior to the intensity measurements.

_exptl_crystal.size_max.name: _exptl_crystal.size_max
_exptl_crystal.size_max.description:
The maximum dimension of the crystal. This item may appear in a
               list with _exptl_crystal.id if multiple crystals are used in the

_exptl_crystal.size_mid.name: _exptl_crystal.size_mid
_exptl_crystal.size_mid.description:

_exptl_crystal.size_min.name: _exptl_crystal.size_min
_exptl_crystal.size_min.description:
The minimum dimension of the crystal. This item may appear in a

_exptl_crystal.size_rad.name: _exptl_crystal.size_rad

The radius of the crystal, if the crystal is a sphere or a

_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_chi.description:

_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_kappa.name: _exptl_crystal_face.diffr_kappa
_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_kappa.description:
The kappa diffractometer setting angle in degrees for a specific

_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_phi.name: _exptl_crystal_face.diffr_phi

_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_psi.name: _exptl_crystal_face.diffr_psi
_exptl_crystal_face.diffr_psi.description:
The psi diffractometer setting angle in degrees for a specific

_exptl_crystal_face.index_h.name: _exptl_crystal_face.index_h
_exptl_crystal_face.index_h.description:

_exptl_crystal_face.index_k.name: _exptl_crystal_face.index_k
_exptl_crystal_face.index_k.description:
Miller index k of the crystal face associated with the value

_exptl_crystal_face.index_l.name: _exptl_crystal_face.index_l
_exptl_crystal_face.index_l.description:

_exptl_crystal_grow.apparatus.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.apparatus
_exptl_crystal_grow.apparatus.description: "              The physical apparatus in which the crystal was grown."

_exptl_crystal_grow.atmosphere.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.atmosphere
_exptl_crystal_grow.atmosphere.description:

_exptl_crystal_grow.crystal_id.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.crystal_id
_exptl_crystal_grow.crystal_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _exptl_crystal.id in the
               EXPTL_CRYSTAL category.

_exptl_crystal_grow.details.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.details
_exptl_crystal_grow.details.description: "              A description of special aspects of the crystal growth."
examples:
Solution 2 was prepared as a well solution and
                                  mixed. A droplet containing 2 \ml of solution
                                  1 was delivered onto a cover slip; 2 \ml of
                                  solution 2 was added to the droplet without
                                  mixing.

Crystal plates were originally stored at room
                                  temperature for 1 week but no nucleation
                                  occurred. They were then transferred to 4
                                  degrees C, at which temperature well formed
                                  single crystals grew in 2 days.

The dependence on pH for successful crystal
                                  growth is very sharp. At pH 7.4 only showers
                                  of tiny crystals grew, at pH 7.5 well formed
                                  single crystals grew, at pH 7.6 no
                                  crystallization occurred at all.

_exptl_crystal_grow.method.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.method
_exptl_crystal_grow.method.description: "              The method used to grow the crystals."

_exptl_crystal_grow.method_ref.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.method_ref
_exptl_crystal_grow.method_ref.description:
A literature reference that describes the method used to grow
               the crystals.

_exptl_crystal_grow.pH.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.pH
_exptl_crystal_grow.pH.description:
The pH at which the crystal was grown. If more than one pH was
               employed during the crystallization process, the final pH should
               be noted here and the protocol involving multiple pH values
               should be described in _exptl_crystal_grow.details.

_exptl_crystal_grow.pressure.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.pressure
_exptl_crystal_grow.pressure.description:
The ambient pressure in kilopascals at which the crystal was
               grown.

_exptl_crystal_grow.pressure_esd.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.pressure_esd
_exptl_crystal_grow.pressure_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _exptl_crystal_grow.pressure.

_exptl_crystal_grow.seeding.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.seeding
_exptl_crystal_grow.seeding.description:
A description of the protocol used for seeding the crystal
               growth.
examples:
Microcrystals were introduced from a previous
                                  crystal growth experiment by transfer with a
                                  human hair.

_exptl_crystal_grow.seeding_ref.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.seeding_ref
_exptl_crystal_grow.seeding_ref.description:
A literature reference that describes the protocol used to seed
               the crystal.

_exptl_crystal_grow.temp_details.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.temp_details
_exptl_crystal_grow.temp_details.description:
A description of special aspects of temperature control during
               crystal growth.

_exptl_crystal_grow.temp_esd.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.temp_esd
_exptl_crystal_grow.temp_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _exptl_crystal_grow.temp.

_exptl_crystal_grow.time.name: _exptl_crystal_grow.time
_exptl_crystal_grow.time.description:
The approximate time that the crystal took to grow to the size
               used for data collection.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.conc.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.conc
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.conc.description: "              The concentration of the solution component."

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.details.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.details
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.details.description:
A description of any special aspects of the solution component.
               When the solution component is the one that contains the
               macromolecule, this could be the specification of the buffer in
               which the macromolecule was stored. When the solution component
               is a buffer component, this could be the methods (or formula)
               used to achieve a desired pH.
examples:
The protein solution was in a buffer
                                  containing 25 mM NaCl, 100 mM NaMES/MES
                                  buffer, pH 7.5, 3 mM NaAzide

in 3 mM NaAzide. Buffer components were mixed
                                  to produce a pH of 4.7 according to a ratio
                                  calculated from the pKa. The actual pH of
                                  solution 2 was not measured.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.crystal_id.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.crystal_id
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.crystal_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _exptl_crystal.id in the
               EXPTL_CRYSTAL category.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.id.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.id
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.id.description:
The value of _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.id must uniquely identify
               each item in the EXPTL_CRYSTAL_GROW_COMP list.

               Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique
               identifier.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.name.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.name
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.name.description: "              A common name for the component of the solution."

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.sol_id.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.sol_id
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.sol_id.description:
An identifier for the solution to which the given solution
               component belongs.

_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.volume.name: _exptl_crystal_grow_comp.volume
_exptl_crystal_grow_comp.volume.description: "              The volume of the solution component."

_geom.entry_id.name: _geom.entry_id
_geom.entry_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category."

_geom.details.name: _geom.details
_geom.details.description:
A description of geometry not covered by the
               existing data names in the GEOM categories, such as
               least-squares planes.

_phasing.method.description:
A listing of the method or methods used to phase this
               structure.

_phasing_averaging.details.name: _phasing_averaging.details
_phasing_averaging.details.description: "              A description of special aspects of the averaging process."

_phasing_averaging.entry_id.name: _phasing_averaging.entry_id
_phasing_averaging.entry_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category."

_phasing_averaging.method.name: _phasing_averaging.method
_phasing_averaging.method.description:
A description of the phase-averaging phasing method used to
               phase this structure.

               Note that this is not the computer program used, which is
               described in the SOFTWARE category, but rather the method
               itself.

               This data item should be used to describe significant
               methodological options used within the phase-averaging program.

_phasing_isomorphous.details.name: _phasing_isomorphous.details
_phasing_isomorphous.details.description: "              A description of special aspects of the isomorphous phasing."
examples:
Residues 13-18 were eliminated from the
                                  starting model as it was anticipated that
                                  binding of the inhibitor would cause a
                                  structural rearrangement in this part of the
                                  structure.

_phasing_isomorphous.entry_id.name: _phasing_isomorphous.entry_id
_phasing_isomorphous.entry_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category."

_phasing_isomorphous.method.name: _phasing_isomorphous.method
_phasing_isomorphous.method.description:
A description of the isomorphous-phasing method used to
               phase this structure.

               Note that this is not the computer program used, which is
               described in the SOFTWARE category, but rather the method
               itself.

               This data item should be used to describe significant
               methodological options used within the isomorphous phasing
               program.
examples:
Iterative threefold averaging alternating with
                                  phase extension by 0.5 reciprocal lattice
                                  units per cycle.

_phasing_isomorphous.parent.name: _phasing_isomorphous.parent
_phasing_isomorphous.parent.description:
Reference to the structure used to generate starting phases
               if the structure referenced in this data block was phased
               by virtue of being isomorphous to a known structure (e.g.
               a mutant that crystallizes in the same space group as the
               wild-type protein.)

_phasing_MAD.details.name: _phasing_MAD.details
_phasing_MAD.details.description: "              A description of special aspects of the MAD phasing."

_phasing_MAD.entry_id.name: _phasing_MAD.entry_id
_phasing_MAD.entry_id.description: "              This data item is a pointer to _entry.id in the ENTRY category."

_phasing_MAD.method.description:

               this structure.

               Note that this is not the computer program used, which is
               described in the SOFTWARE category, but rather the method
               itself.

               This data item should be used to describe significant
               methodological options used within the MAD phasing program.

               PHASING_MAD_EXPT category.

_phasing_MAD_clust.number_set.name: _phasing_MAD_clust.number_set
_phasing_MAD_clust.number_set.description: "              The number of data sets in this cluster of data sets."

_phasing_MAD_expt.delta_delta_phi.description:
The difference between two independent determinations of
               _phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi.

_phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi.name: _phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi
_phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi.description:
The phase difference between F~t~(h), the structure factor due
               to normal scattering from all atoms, and F~a~(h), the structure
               factor due to normal scattering from only the anomalous
               scatterers.

_phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi_sigma.name: _phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi_sigma
_phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi_sigma.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _phasing_MAD_expt.delta_phi.

_phasing_MAD_expt.number_clust.description: "              The number of clusters of data sets in this phasing experiment."

_phasing_MAD_ratio.d_res_high.name: _phasing_MAD_ratio.d_res_high
_phasing_MAD_ratio.d_res_high.description:
The lowest value for the interplanar spacings for the
               reflection data used for the comparison of Bijvoet differences.
               This is called the highest resolution.

_phasing_MAD_ratio.d_res_low.name: _phasing_MAD_ratio.d_res_low
_phasing_MAD_ratio.d_res_low.description:
The highest value for the interplanar spacings for the
               reflection data used for the comparison of Bijvoet differences.
               This is called the lowest resolution.

_phasing_MAD_ratio.expt_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _phasing_MAD_expt.id in the
               PHASING_MAD_EXPT category.

_phasing_MAD_ratio.clust_id.description:

               the PHASING_MAD_CLUST category.

_phasing_MAD_ratio.ratio_one_wl.name: _phasing_MAD_ratio.ratio_one_wl
_phasing_MAD_ratio.ratio_one_wl.description:
The root-mean-square Bijvoet difference at one wavelength for
               all reflections.

_phasing_MAD_ratio.ratio_one_wl_centric.name: _phasing_MAD_ratio.ratio_one_wl_centric
_phasing_MAD_ratio.ratio_one_wl_centric.description:
The root-mean-square Bijvoet difference at one wavelength for
               centric reflections. This would be equal to zero for perfect
               data and thus serves as an estimate of the noise in the
               anomalous signals.

_phasing_MAD_ratio.ratio_two_wl.name: _phasing_MAD_ratio.ratio_two_wl
_phasing_MAD_ratio.ratio_two_wl.description:
The root-mean-square dispersive Bijvoet difference between
               two wavelengths for all reflections.

_phasing_MAD_ratio.wavelength_1.description:

_phasing_MAD_set.d_res_high.name: _phasing_MAD_set.d_res_high
_phasing_MAD_set.d_res_high.description:
The lowest value for the interplanar spacings for the
               reflection data used for this set of data. This is called
               the highest resolution.

_phasing_MAD_set.d_res_low.description:
The highest value for the interplanar spacings for the
               reflection data used for this set of data. This is called

The mean value of the figure of merit m for all reflections

                   int P~alpha~ exp(i*alpha) dalpha

               Note that this is not the computer program used, which is
               described in the SOFTWARE category, but rather the method
               itself.

               This data item should be used to describe significant
               methodological options used within the MIR phasing program.

_phasing_MIR_der.details.description:

_phasing_MIR_der.native_set_id.name: _phasing_MIR_der.native_set_id
_phasing_MIR_der.native_set_id.description:

               This data item is a pointer to _phasing_set.id in the

_phasing_MIR_der.number_of_sites.name: _phasing_MIR_der.number_of_sites
_phasing_MIR_der.number_of_sites.description: "              The number of heavy-atom sites in this derivative."

_phasing_MIR_der.power_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.power_acentric
_phasing_MIR_der.power_acentric.description:
The mean phasing power P for acentric reflections for this
               derivative.

                          sum|Fh~calc~^2^|
               P = (----------------------------)^1/2^
                    sum|Fph~obs~ - Fph~calc~|^2^

               Fph~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of this
                           derivative
               Fph~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude of this
                           derivative
               Fh~calc~  = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the
                           heavy-atom model

               sum is taken over the specified reflections

_phasing_MIR_der.power_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.power_centric
_phasing_MIR_der.power_centric.description:
The mean phasing power P for centric reflections for this
               derivative.

                          sum|Fh~calc~^2^|
               P = (----------------------------)^1/2^
                    sum|Fph~obs~ - Fph~calc~|^2^

               Fph~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                           derivative
               Fph~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude of the
                           derivative
               Fh~calc~  = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the
                           heavy-atom model

               sum is taken over the specified reflections

_phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_acentric
_phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_acentric.description:
Residual factor R~cullis,acen~ for acentric reflections for this
               derivative.

               The Cullis R factor was originally defined only for centric
               reflections.  It is, however, also a useful statistical
               measure for acentric reflections, which is how it is used in
               this data item.

                           sum| |Fph~obs~ +/- Fp~obs~| - Fh~calc~ |
          R~cullis,acen~ = ----------------------------------------
                                   sum|Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~|

               Fp~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the native
               Fph~obs~ = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                          derivative
               Fh~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the
                          heavy-atom model

               sum is taken over the specified reflections

               Ref: Cullis, A. F., Muirhead, H., Perutz, M. F., Rossmann, M. G.
                    & North, A. C. T. (1961). Proc. R. Soc. London Ser. A,
                    265, 15-38.

_phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_anomalous.name: _phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_anomalous
_phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_anomalous.description:
Residual factor R~cullis,ano~ for anomalous reflections for this
               derivative.

               The Cullis R factor was originally defined only for centric
               reflections.  It is, however, also a useful statistical
               measure for anomalous  reflections, which is how it is used in
               this data item.

               This is tabulated for acentric terms.  A value less than 1.0
               means there is some contribution to the phasing from the
               anomalous data.

                            sum |Fph+~obs~Fph-~obs~ - Fh+~calc~ - Fh-~calc~|
            R~cullis,ano~ = ------------------------------------------------
                                   sum|Fph+~obs~ - Fph-~obs~|

               Fph+~obs~  = the observed positive Friedel structure-factor
                            amplitude for the derivative
               Fph-~obs~  = the observed negative Friedel structure-factor
                            amplitude for the derivative

               Fh+~calc~  = the calculated positive Friedel structure-factor
                            amplitude from the heavy-atom model
               Fh-~calc~  = the calculated negative Friedel structure-factor
                            amplitude from the heavy-atom model

               sum is taken over the specified reflections

               Ref: Cullis, A. F., Muirhead, H., Perutz, M. F., Rossmann, M. G.
                    & North, A. C. T. (1961). Proc. R. Soc.  London Ser. A,
                    265, 15-38.

_phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_centric
_phasing_MIR_der.R_cullis_centric.description:
Residual factor R~cullis~ for centric reflections for this
               derivative.

                           sum| |Fph~obs~ +/- Fp~obs~| - Fh~calc~ |
               R~cullis~ = ----------------------------------------
                                   sum|Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~|

               Fp~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the native
               Fph~obs~ = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                          derivative
               Fh~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the
                          heavy-atom model

               sum is taken over the specified reflections

               Ref: Cullis, A. F., Muirhead, H., Perutz, M. F., Rossmann, M. G.
                    & North, A. C. T. (1961). Proc. R. Soc. London Ser. A,
                    265, 15-38.

_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.reflns_acentric
_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_acentric.description:
The number of acentric reflections used in phasing for this
               derivative.

_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_anomalous.name: _phasing_MIR_der.reflns_anomalous
_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_anomalous.description:

_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_der.reflns_centric
_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_centric.description:
The number of centric reflections used in phasing for this

_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_criteria.name: _phasing_MIR_der.reflns_criteria
_phasing_MIR_der.reflns_criteria.description:

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.der_id.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.der_id
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.der_id.description:

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_calc.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_calc
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_calc.description:

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_calc_au.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_calc_au
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_calc_au.description:

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas.description:
The measured value of the structure factor for this derivative,
               in electrons.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_au.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_au
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_au.description:
The measured value of the structure factor for this derivative,
               in arbitrary units.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_sigma.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_sigma
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_sigma.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas, in electrons.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_sigma_au.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_sigma_au
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_sigma_au.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _phasing_MIR_der_refln.F_meas_au, in arbitrary units.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_A_iso.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_A_iso
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_A_iso.description:
The isomorphous Hendrickson-Lattman coefficient A~iso~ for this
               reflection for this derivative.

                         -2.0 * (Fp~obs~^2^ + Fh~calc~^2^ - Fph~obs~^2^)
                              *  Fp~obs~ * cos(alphah~calc~)
               A~iso~ =  -----------------------------------------------
                                              E^2^

               E            = (Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~ - Fh~calc~)^2^
                              for centric reflections
                            = [(Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~) * 2^1/2^ - Fh~calc~]^2^
                              for acentric reflections

               Fp~obs~      = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                              native
               Fph~obs~     = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                              derivative
               Fh~calc~     = the calculated structure-factor amplitude
                              from the heavy-atom model
               alphah~calc~ = the calculated phase from the heavy-atom model

               This coefficient appears in the expression for the phase
               probability of each isomorphous derivative:

               P~i~(alpha) = exp[k + A * cos(alpha) + B * sin(alpha)
                                   + C * cos(2 * alpha) + D * sin(2 * alpha)]

               Ref: Hendrickson, W. A. & Lattman, E. E. (1970). Acta
                    Cryst. B26, 136-143.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_B_iso.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_B_iso
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_B_iso.description:
The isomorphous Hendrickson-Lattman coefficient B~iso~ for this
               reflection for this derivative.

                         -2.0 * (Fp~obs~^2^ + Fh~calc~^2^ - Fph~obs~^2^)
                              *  Fp~obs~ * sin(alphah~calc~)
               B~iso~ =  -----------------------------------------------
                                              E^2^

               E            = (Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~ - Fh~calc~)^2^
                              for centric reflections
                            = [(Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~) * 2^1/2^ - Fh~calc~]^2^
                              for acentric reflections

               Fp~obs~      = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                              native
               Fph~obs~     = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                              derivative
               Fh~calc~     = the calculated structure-factor amplitude
                              from the heavy-atom model
               alphah~calc~ = the phase calculated from the heavy-atom model

               This coefficient appears in the expression for the phase
               probability of each isomorphous derivative:

               P~i~(alpha) = exp[k + A * cos(alpha) + B * sin(alpha)
                                   + C * cos(2 * alpha) + D * sin(2 * alpha)]

               Ref: Hendrickson, W. A. & Lattman, E. E. (1970). Acta
                    Cryst. B26, 136-143.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_C_iso.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_C_iso
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_C_iso.description:
The isomorphous Hendrickson-Lattman coefficient C~iso~ for this
               reflection for this derivative.

                         -Fp~obs~^2^ * [sin(alphah~calc~)^2^
                                     -  cos(alphah~calc~)^2^]
               C~iso~ =  ------------------------------------
                                        E^2^

               E            = (Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~ - Fh~calc~)^2^
                              for centric reflections
                            = [(Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~) * 2^1/2^ - Fh~calc~]^2^
                              for acentric reflections

               Fp~obs~      = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                              native
               Fph~obs~     = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                              derivative
               Fh~calc~     = the calculated structure-factor amplitude
                              from the heavy-atom model
               alphah~calc~ = the phase calculated from the heavy-atom model

               This coefficient appears in the expression for the phase
               probability of each isomorphous derivative:

               P~i~(alpha) = exp[k + A * cos(alpha) + B * sin(alpha)
                                   + C * cos(2 * alpha) + D * sin(2 * alpha)]

               Ref: Hendrickson, W. A. & Lattman, E. E. (1970). Acta
                    Cryst. B26, 136-143.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_D_iso.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_D_iso
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.HL_D_iso.description:
The isomorphous Hendrickson-Lattman coefficient D~iso~ for this
               reflection for this derivative.

                         -2.0 * Fp~obs~^2^ * sin(alphah~calc~)^2^
                                           * cos(alphah~calc~)^2^
               D~iso~ =  ----------------------------------------
                                          E^2^

               E            = (Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~ - Fh~calc~)^2^
                              for centric reflections
                            = [(Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~) * 2^1/2^ - Fh~calc~]^2^
                              for acentric reflections

               Fp~obs~      = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                              native
               Fph~obs~     = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                              derivative
               Fh~calc~     = the calculated structure-factor amplitude
                              from the heavy-atom model
               alphah~calc~ = the phase calculated from the heavy-atom model

               This coefficient appears in the expression for the phase
               probability of each isomorphous derivative:

               P~i~(alpha) = exp[k + A * cos(alpha) + B * sin(alpha)
                                   + C * cos(2 * alpha) + D * sin(2 * alpha)]

               Ref: Hendrickson, W. A. & Lattman, E. E. (1970). Acta
                    Cryst. B26, 136-143.

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.index_h.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.index_h
_phasing_MIR_der_refln.index_h.description: "              Miller index h for this reflection for this derivative."

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.index_k.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.index_k

_phasing_MIR_der_refln.set_id.name: _phasing_MIR_der_refln.set_id

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.d_res_low.description:

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.der_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _phasing_MIR_der.id in the
               PHASING_MIR_DER category.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.fom.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.fom
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.fom.description:
The mean value of the figure of merit m for reflections for this
               derivative in this shell.

                   int P~alpha~ exp(i*alpha) dalpha
               m = --------------------------------
                         int P~alpha~ dalpha

               P~alpha~ = the probability that the phase angle alpha is correct

               int is taken over the range alpha = 0 to 2 pi.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.ha_ampl.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.ha_ampl

               derivative in this shell.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.loc.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.loc
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.loc.description:
The mean lack-of-closure error loc for reflections for this
               derivative in this shell.

               loc = sum|Fph~obs~ - Fph~calc~|

               Fph~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                           derivative
               Fph~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude of the
                           derivative

               sum is taken over the specified reflections

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.phase.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.phase
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.phase.description:
The mean of the phase values for reflections for this
               derivative in this shell.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.power.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.power
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.power.description:
The mean phasing power P for reflections for this derivative
               in this shell.

                          sum|Fh~calc~^2^|
               P = (----------------------------)^1/2^
                    sum|Fph~obs~ - Fph~calc~|^2^

               Fph~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                           derivative
               Fph~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude of the
                           derivative
               Fh~calc~  = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the
                           heavy-atom model

               sum is taken over the specified reflections

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.R_cullis.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.R_cullis
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.R_cullis.description:
Residual factor R~cullis~ for centric reflections for this
               derivative in this shell.

                           sum| |Fph~obs~ +/- Fp~obs~| - Fh~calc~ |
               R~cullis~ = ----------------------------------------
                                   sum|Fph~obs~ - Fp~obs~|

               Fp~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the native
               Fph~obs~ = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                          derivative
               Fh~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude from the
                          heavy-atom model

               sum is taken over the specified reflections

               Ref: Cullis, A. F., Muirhead, H., Perutz, M. F., Rossmann, M. G.
                    & North, A. C. T. (1961). Proc. R. Soc. London Ser. A,
                    265, 15-38.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.R_kraut.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.R_kraut
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.R_kraut.description:
Residual factor R~kraut~ for general reflections for this
               derivative in this shell.

                          sum|Fph~obs~ - Fph~calc~|
               R~kraut~ = -------------------------
                                sum|Fph~obs~|

               Fph~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitude of the
                           derivative
               Fph~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitude of the
                           derivative

               sum is taken over the specified reflections

               Ref: Kraut, J., Sieker, L. C., High, D. F. & Freer, S. T.
                    (1962). Proc. Natl Acad. Sci. USA, 48, 1417-1424.

_phasing_MIR_der_shell.reflns.name: _phasing_MIR_der_shell.reflns
_phasing_MIR_der_shell.reflns.description: "              The number of reflections in this shell."

_phasing_MIR_der_site.B_iso.description:

_phasing_MIR_der_site.B_iso_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.B_iso_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.B_iso_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _phasing_MIR_der_site.B_iso.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x
_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x.description:
The x coordinate of this heavy-atom position in this derivative
               specified as orthogonal angstroms. The orthogonal Cartesian axes
               are related to the cell axes as specified by the description
               given in _atom_sites.Cartn_transform_axes.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_x.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y
_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y.description:
The y coordinate of this heavy-atom position in this derivative
               specified as orthogonal angstroms. The orthogonal Cartesian axes
               are related to the cell axes as specified by the description
               given in _atom_sites.Cartn_transform_axes.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_y.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z
_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z.description:
The z coordinate of this heavy-atom position in this derivative
               specified as orthogonal angstroms. The orthogonal Cartesian axes
               are related to the cell axes as specified by the description
               given in _atom_sites.Cartn_transform_axes.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _phasing_MIR_der_site.Cartn_z.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.der_id.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.der_id
_phasing_MIR_der_site.der_id.description:
This data item is a pointer to _phasing_MIR_der.id in the
               PHASING_MIR_DER category.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.details.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.details
_phasing_MIR_der_site.details.description: "              A description of special aspects of the derivative site."
examples: "same as site 2 in the K2HgI4 derivative"

_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x
_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x.description:
The x coordinate of this heavy-atom position in this derivative
               specified as a fraction of _cell.length_a.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_x.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y
_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y.description:
The y coordinate of this heavy-atom position in this derivative
               specified as a fraction of _cell.length_b.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_y.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z
_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z.description:
The z coordinate of this heavy-atom position in this derivative
               specified as a fraction of _cell.length_c.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z_esd.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z_esd
_phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z_esd.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of _phasing_MIR_der_site.fract_z.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.id.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.id
_phasing_MIR_der_site.id.description:
The value of _phasing_MIR_der_site.id must uniquely identify each
               site in each derivative in the PHASING_MIR_DER_SITE list.

               The atom identifiers need not be unique over all sites in all
               derivatives; they need only be unique for each site in each
               derivative.

               Note that this item need not be a number; it can be any unique
               identifier.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy
_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy.description:
The fraction of the atom type present at this heavy-atom site
               in a given derivative. The sum of the occupancies of all the
               atom types at this site may not significantly exceed 1.0 unless
               it is a dummy site.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom
_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom.description:
The relative anomalous occupancy of the atom type
               present at this heavy-atom site in a given derivative.
               This atom occupancy will probably be on an arbitrary scale.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom_su.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom_su
_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom_su.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of
               _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_anom.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso
_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso.description:
The relative real isotropic occupancy of the atom type
               present at this heavy-atom site in a given derivative.
               This atom occupancy will probably be on an arbitrary scale.

_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso_su.name: _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso_su
_phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso_su.description:
The standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation) of
               _phasing_MIR_der_site.occupancy_iso.

_phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_high.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_high
_phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_high.description:
The lowest value for the interplanar spacings for the
               reflection data in this shell. This is called the highest
               resolution. Note that the resolution limits of shells in
               the items _phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_high and
               _phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_low are independent of the resolution
               limits of shells in the items _reflns_shell.d_res_high and
               _reflns_shell.d_res_low.

_phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_low.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_low
_phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_low.description:
The highest value for the interplanar spacings for the
               reflection data in this shell. This is called the lowest
               resolution. Note that the resolution limits of shells in the
               items _phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_high and
               _phasing_MIR_shell.d_res_low are independent of the resolution
               limits of shells in the items _reflns_shell.d_res_high and
               _reflns_shell.d_res_low.

_phasing_MIR_shell.FOM.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.FOM
_phasing_MIR_shell.FOM.description:
The mean value of the figure of merit m for reflections in this
               shell.

                   int P~alpha~ exp(i*alpha) dalpha
               m = --------------------------------
                         int P~alpha~ dalpha

               P~alpha~ = the probability that the phase angle alpha is correct

               the integral is taken over the range alpha = 0 to 2 pi.

_phasing_MIR_shell.FOM_acentric.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.FOM_acentric
_phasing_MIR_shell.FOM_acentric.description:
The mean value of the figure of merit m for acentric reflections
               in this shell.

                   int P~alpha~ exp(i*alpha) dalpha
               m = --------------------------------
                         int P~alpha~ dalpha

               P~a~ = the probability that the phase angle a is correct

               the integral is taken over the range alpha = 0 to 2 pi.

_phasing_MIR_shell.FOM_centric.name: _phasing_MIR_shell.FOM_centric
_phasing_MIR_shell.FOM_centric.description:
The mean value of the figure of merit m for centric reflections
               in this shell.

                   int P~alpha~ exp(i*alpha) dalpha
               m = --------------------------------
                         int P~alpha~ dalpha

               P~a~ = the probability that the phase angle a is correct
