_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.chem_shift_val.description: Value assigned to the chemical shift of the reference compound.

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.correction_val.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.correction_val
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.correction_val.description:
An uniform correction value that was applied because of an extenuating
circumstance such as data collection at an unusual temperature.

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.external_ref_ax.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.external_ref_axis
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.external_ref_ax.description:
The axis of the external chemical shift reference sample relative to the
static field (Bo) of the spectrometer.

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.external_ref_lo.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.external_ref_loc
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.external_ref_lo.description:
External chemical shift references are defined as either located within
the sample (e.g., as a capillary) or external to the sample and are inserted into
the spectrometer before, after, or both before and after the sample containing
the molecular system studied in the entry.
examples: "insert at center of experimental sample tube"

enumeration.value: "insert at center of experimental sample tube"
"insert at outer edge of experimental sample tube"
"insert at center of a separate sample tube"
"insert at outer edge of a separate sample tube"
"separate tube (no insert) similar to the experimental sample tube"
"separate tube (no insert) not similar to the experimental sample tube"

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.external_ref_sa.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.external_ref_sample_geometry
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.external_ref_sa.description: The geometrical shape of the external reference sample.

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.indirect_shift_.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.indirect_shift_ratio
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.indirect_shift_.description:
The Chi value used in calculating the chemical shift referencing values
for nuclei that are referenced indirectly.  The values used should be those recommended
by the IUPAC Taskforce on the deposition of data to the public databases
(Markley, et al.  Pure and Appl. Chem. 70, 117-142 (1998).

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.mol_common_name.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.mol_common_name
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.mol_common_name.description:
Common name or abbreviation used in the literature for the molecule used
as a chemical shift reference.

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.rank.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.rank
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.rank.description:
The rank of the chemical shift reference. A primary reference is the one
used in reporting the data. A secondary reference would be the compound in the
sample or used as an external reference.

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.ref_correction_.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.ref_correction_type
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.ref_correction_.description:
If a correction value is applied to calculate the reported chemical shifts
the source of the correction (pH; temperature; etc.).

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.ref_method.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.ref_method
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.ref_method.description:
The chemical shift reference may be either internal (the compound is located
in the sample) or external (the compound is in a container external to the sample).

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.ref_type.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.ref_type
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.ref_type.description:
The reference type may be either direct (against a value measured with
a chemical compound) or indirect (calculated from chemical shift ratios).

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.solvent.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.solvent
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_ref.solvent.description: Solvent used for the external reference sample.

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.carbon_sh.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.carbon_shifts_flag
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.carbon_sh.description:
A value indicating if 13C chemical shifts are being deposited and if IUPAC chemical shift referencing was used. This item
is a user interface item that is used to trigger the automated population of chemical shift referencing tags if IUPAC
chemical shift referencing parameters have been used.

enumeration.detail: "13C chemical shifts are not being deposited"
"13C chemical shifts are being deposited and the shifts are referenced using the IUPAC recommendations"

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.details.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.details
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.details.description:
Text providing additional information regarding the reported chemical shift
referencing values or methods.

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.id.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.id
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.id.description:
An integer value that uniquely identifies a set of chemical shift
reference values from other sets of chemical shift referencing values.

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.label.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.label
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.label.description:
A descriptive label that uniquely identifies this list of chemical shift
referencing parameters used in reporting assigned chemical shifts and other chemical
shift data.

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.nitrogen_.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.nitrogen_shifts_flag
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.nitrogen_.description:
A value indicating if 15N chemical shifts are being deposited and if IUPAC chemical shift referencing was used. This item
is a user interface item that is used to trigger the automated population of chemical shift referencing tags if IUPAC
chemical shift referencing parameters have been used.

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.other_shi.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.other_shifts_flag
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.other_shi.description: A value indicating if chemical shifts other than 1H, 13C, 15N, or 31P are being deposited.

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.phosphoru.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.phosphorus_shifts_flag
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.phosphoru.description:
A value indicating if 31P chemical shifts are being deposited and if IUPAC chemical shift referencing was used. This item
is a user interface item that is used to trigger the automated population of chemical shift referencing tags if IUPAC
chemical shift referencing parameters have been used.

_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.proton_sh.name: _pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.proton_shifts_flag
_pdbx_nmr_chem_shift_reference.proton_sh.description:
A value indicating if 1H chemical shifts are being deposited and if IUPAC chemical shift referencing was used. This item
is a user interface item that is used to trigger the automated population of chemical shift referencing tags if IUPAC
chemical shift referencing parameters have been used.
Please indicate yes or no$ if you are depositing 1H chemical shifts.

_pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.constraint_fil.name: _pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.constraint_filename
_pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.constraint_fil.description: Name of the uploaded file that contains the constraint data.

_pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.constraint_num.name: _pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.constraint_number
_pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.constraint_num.description: Number of constraints of this type and subtype in the file.

_pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.constraint_typ.name: _pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.constraint_type
_pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.constraint_typ.description: The general type of constraint (distance, torsion angle, RDC, etc.)

_pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.id.name: _pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.id
_pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.id.description: Local unique identifier for the listed constraint file.

_pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.software_name.name: _pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.software_name
_pdbx_nmr_constraint_file.software_name.description:
Name of the software application that the listed constraint file is used
as input.

_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.detail.name: _pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.details
_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.detail.description:
General details describing conditions of both the sample and the environment
during measurements.
examples: The high salinity of the sample may have contributed to overheating of the sample during experiments with long saturation periods like the TOCSY experiments.

_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.ionic_.name: _pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.ionic_strength_err
_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.ionic_.description: Estimate of the standard error for the value for the sample ionic strength.

_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.ionic_.name: _pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.ionic_strength_units
_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.ionic_.description: Units for the value of the sample condition ionic strength..

_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.label.name: _pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.label
_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.label.description: A descriptive label that uniquely identifies this set of sample conditions.

_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.pH_err.name: _pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.pH_err
_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.pH_err.description: Estimate of the standard error for the value for the sample pH.

_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.pH_uni.name: _pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.pH_units
_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.pH_uni.description: Units for the value of the sample condition pH.

_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.pressu.name: _pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.pressure_err
_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.pressu.description: Estimate of the standard error for the value for the sample pressure.

_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.temper.name: _pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.temperature_err
_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.temper.description: Estimate of the standard error for the value for the sample temperature.

_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.temper.name: _pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.temperature_units
_pdbx_nmr_exptl_sample_conditions.temper.description: Units for the value of the sample condition temperature.

_pdbx_nmr_exptl.sample_state.name: _pdbx_nmr_exptl.sample_state
_pdbx_nmr_exptl.sample_state.description: Physical state of the sample either anisotropic or isotropic.

_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.label.name: _pdbx_nmr_sample_details.label
_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.label.description:
A value that uniquely identifies this sample from the other samples listed
in the entry.

_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.type.name: _pdbx_nmr_sample_details.type
_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.type.description:
A descriptive term for the sample that defines the general physical properties
of the sample.

_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.details.name: _pdbx_nmr_sample_details.details
_pdbx_nmr_sample_details.details.description: Brief description of the sample providing additional information not captured by other items in the category.
examples: The added glycerol was used to raise the viscosity of the solution to 1.05 poisson.

_pdbx_nmr_software_task.task.name: _pdbx_nmr_software_task.task
_pdbx_nmr_software_task.task.description:
A word or brief phrase that describes the task that a software application
was used to carry out.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.id.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.id
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.id.description:
An integer value that specifies the dimension of a multidimensional NMR
spectrum.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.atom_type.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.atom_type
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.atom_type.description:
The value for this tag is a standard IUPAC abbreviation for an element
(i.e., H, C, N, P, etc).

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.atom_isotope_numb.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.atom_isotope_number
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.atom_isotope_numb.description: The mass number for the specified atom.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.spectral_region.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.spectral_region
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.spectral_region.description:
A code defining the type of nuclei that would be expected to be observed
in the spectral region observed in the dimension of the spectrum.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.magnetization_lin.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.magnetization_linkage_id
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.magnetization_lin.description:
The magnetization linkage ID is used to designate dimensions of a multidimensional
NMR experiment where the nuclei observed in the dimensions are directly
linked by a one bond scalar coupling. For example, the amide proton and amide
nitrogen in an 1H-15N HSQC experiment.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.sweep_width.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.sweep_width
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.sweep_width.description: The width of the spectral window observed in Hz.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.encoding_code.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.encoding_code
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.encoding_code.description:
Code describing how information from one spectral dimension has been encoded
in another dimension in for example a reduced dimensionality experiment.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.encoded_source_di.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.encoded_source_dimension_id
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.encoded_source_di.description:
Pointer to '_pdbx_nmr_spectral_dim.id'. The spectral dimension in a reduced dimensionality experiment that is the
source of the magnetization that has been encoded.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.data_file_n.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.data_file_name
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.data_file_n.description:
The name of the file submitted with a deposition that contains the quantitative
data for a spectral peak list.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.number_of_s.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.number_of_spectral_dimensions
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.number_of_s.description: Number of dimension in the spectrum from which the peak list was extracted.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.details.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.details
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.details.description: Text describing the reported list of spectral peaks.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.text_data_f.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.text_data_format
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.text_data_f.description:
The data format used to represent the spectral peak data as ASCII text
in the text block that is the value to the '_Spectral_peak_list.Text_data' tag.

_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.details.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.details
_pdbx_nmr_spectrometer.details.description: A text description of the NMR spectrometer.

_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.t.name: _pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.type
_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.t.description:
The kind of chemical shift offset that should be applied to all chemical
shifts observed for a specific type of atom.

_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.a.name: _pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.atom_type
_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.a.description: The kind of atom to which the chemical shift offset value applies.

_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.a.name: _pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.atom_isotope_number
_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.a.description:
Mass number for the atom defined by the '.Atom_type' tag with a systematic
chemical shift offset.

_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.v.name: _pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.val
_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.v.description:
Chemical shift value that has been applied to all of the reported chemical
shifts to bring their values in line with values expected to be observed for
the chemical shift referencing that was used.

_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.v.name: _pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.val_err
_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.v.description: Error in the reported chemical shift offset value used.

_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.o.name: _pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.ordinal
_pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset.o.description: An ordinal identifier uniquely identifying records in the pdbx_nmr_systematic_chem_shift_offset category.

_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_id.name: _pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_id
_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_id.description:
Unique code assigned to the file being uploaded by the depositor and that
contains data that will be incorporated into this entry.

_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_name.name: _pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_name
_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_name.description: The directory path and file name for the data file that is to be uploaded.

_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_category.name: _pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_category
_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_category.description: This item defines the kind of data in the file uploaded for deposition.

_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_syntax.name: _pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_syntax
_pdbx_nmr_upload.data_file_syntax.description: The syntax or format of the file that is uploaded.

_pdbx_nmr_assigned_chem_shift_list.label.name: _pdbx_nmr_assigned_chem_shift_list.label
_pdbx_nmr_assigned_chem_shift_list.label.description: A descriptive label that uniquely identifies a list of reported assigned chemical shifts.

_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.label.name: _pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.label
_pdbx_nmr_spectral_peak_list.label.description: A descriptive label that uniquely identifies a list of reported spectral peaks.

_pdbx_chem_comp_subcomponent_struct_conn.description: "              The atom identifier for the first atom in the interaction."

_pdbx_chem_comp_subcomponent_struct_conn.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_subcomponent_struct_conn.atom_id_2
_pdbx_chem_comp_subcomponent_struct_conn.description: "              The atom identifier for the second atom in the interaction."

_pdbx_chem_comp_subcomponent_struct_conn.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_subcomponent_struct_conn.comp_id_1
_pdbx_chem_comp_subcomponent_struct_conn.description: "              The component identifier for the first atom in the interaction."

_entity_src_nat.pdbx_alt_source_flag.name: _entity_src_nat.pdbx_alt_source_flag

_entity_src_nat.pdbx_beg_seq_num.description:
The beginning polymer sequence position for the polymer section corresponding

_entity_src_nat.pdbx_end_seq_num.name: _entity_src_nat.pdbx_end_seq_num

The ending polymer sequence position for the polymer section corresponding

_pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_src_id.description: "              This data item is an ordinal identifier for pdbx_entity_src_syn data records."

_pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_alt_source_fla.name: _pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_alt_source_flag
_pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_alt_source_fla.description:
This data item identifies cases in which an alternative source
               modeled.

_pdbx_entity_src_syn.pdbx_beg_seq_num.description:
The beginning polymer sequence position for the polymer section corresponding

_entity_poly.pdbx_seq_one_letter_code_sa.name: _entity_poly.pdbx_seq_one_letter_code_sample
_entity_poly.pdbx_seq_one_letter_code_sa.description:
For cases in which the sample and model sequence differ this item contains
               the sample chemical sequence expressed as string of one-letter amino acid codes.

               Modified may be include as 'X' or with their 3-letter codes in parentheses.
examples:
A  for alanine or adenine
B  for ambiguous asparagine/aspartic-acid
R  for arginine
N  for asparagine
D  for aspartic-acid
C  for cysteine or cystine or cytosine
Q  for glutamine
E  for glutamic-acid
Z  for ambiguous glutamine/glutamic acid
G  for glycine or guanine
H  for histidine
I  for isoleucine
L  for leucine
K  for lysine
M  for methionine
F  for phenylalanine
P  for proline
S  for serine
T  for threonine or thymine
W  for tryptophan
Y  for tyrosine
V  for valine
U  for uracil
O  for water
X  for other

_refine.overall_SU_B.description:
The overall standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of the displacement parameters based on a maximum-likelihood
               residual.

               The overall standard uncertainty (sigma~B~)^2^ gives an idea
               of the uncertainty in the B values of averagely defined
               atoms (atoms with B values equal to the average B value).

                                             N~a~
    (sigma~B~)^2^ = 8 ----------------------------------------------
                      sum~i~ {[1/Sigma - (E~o~)^2^ (1-m^2^)](SUM_AS)s^4^}

               N~a~           = number of atoms
               E~o~           = normalized structure factors
               m              = figure of merit of phases of reflections
                                included in the summation
               s              = reciprocal-space vector

               SUM_AS         = (sigma~A~)^2^/Sigma^2^
               Sigma          = (sigma~{E;exp}~)^2^ + epsilon [1-(sigma~A~)^2^]
               sigma~{E;exp}~  = experimental uncertainties of normalized
                                structure factors
               sigma~A~        = <cos 2 pi s delta~x~> SQRT(Sigma~P~/Sigma~N~)
                                estimated using maximum likelihood
               Sigma~P~        = sum~{atoms in model}~ f^2^
               Sigma~N~        = sum~{atoms in crystal}~ f^2^
               f               = atom form factor
               delta~x~        = expected error
               epsilon         = multiplicity of diffracting plane

               summation is over all reflections included in refinement

               Ref: (sigma~A~ estimation) "Refinement of macromolecular
                    structures by the maximum-likelihood method",
                    Murshudov, G. N., Vagin, A. A. & Dodson, E. J. (1997).
                    Acta Cryst. D53, 240-255.

                    (SU B estimation) Murshudov, G. N. & Dodson,
                    E. J. (1997). Simplified error estimation a la
                    Cruickshank in macromolecular crystallography.
                    CCP4 Newsletter on Protein Crystallography, No. 33,
                    January 1997, pp. 31-39.

                   http://www.ccp4.ac.uk/newsletters/newsletter33/murshudov.html

_refine.overall_SU_ML.name: _refine.overall_SU_ML
_refine.overall_SU_ML.description:
The overall standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of the positional parameters based on a maximum likelihood
               residual.

               The overall standard uncertainty (sigma~X~)^2^ gives an
               idea of the uncertainty in the position of averagely
               defined atoms (atoms with B values equal to average B value)

                     3                         N~a~
    (sigma~X~)^2^  = ---------------------------------------------------------
                     8 pi^2^ sum~i~ {[1/Sigma - (E~o~)^2^ (1-m^2^)](SUM_AS)s^2^}

               N~a~           = number of atoms
               E~o~           = normalized structure factors
               m              = figure of merit of phases of reflections
                                included in the summation
               s              = reciprocal-space vector

               SUM_AS         = (sigma~A~)^2^/Sigma^2^
               Sigma          = (sigma~{E;exp}~)^2^ + epsilon [1-(sigma~A~)^2^]
               sigma~{E;exp}~  = experimental uncertainties of normalized
                                structure factors
               sigma~A~        = <cos 2 pi s delta~x~> SQRT(Sigma~P~/Sigma~N~)
                                estimated using maximum likelihood
               Sigma~P~        = sum~{atoms in model}~ f^2^
               Sigma~N~        = sum~{atoms in crystal}~ f^2^
               f               = atom form factor
               delta~x~        = expected error
               epsilon         = multiplicity of diffracting plane

               summation is over all reflections included in refinement

               Ref: (sigma_A estimation) "Refinement of macromolecular
                    structures by the maximum-likelihood method",
                    Murshudov, G. N., Vagin, A. A. & Dodson, E. J. (1997).
                    Acta Cryst. D53, 240-255.

                    (SU ML estimation) Murshudov, G. N. & Dodson,
                    E. J. (1997). Simplified error estimation a la
                    Cruickshank in macromolecular crystallography.
                    CCP4 Newsletter on Protein Crystallography, No. 33,
                    January 1997, pp. 31-39.

                   http://www.ccp4.ac.uk/newsletters/newsletter33/murshudov.html

_refine.overall_SU_R_Cruickshank_DPI.name: _refine.overall_SU_R_Cruickshank_DPI
_refine.overall_SU_R_Cruickshank_DPI.description:
The overall standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of the displacement parameters based on the crystallographic
               R value, expressed in a formalism known as the dispersion
               precision indicator (DPI).

               The overall standard uncertainty (sigma~B~) gives an idea
               of the uncertainty in the B values of averagely defined
               atoms (atoms with B values equal to the average B value).

                                      N~a~
               (sigma~B~)^2^ = 0.65 ---------- (R~value~)^2^ (D~min~)^2^ C^-2/3^
                                    (N~o~-N~p~)


               N~a~     = number of atoms included in refinement
               N~o~     = number of observations
               N~p~     = number of parameters refined
               R~value~ = conventional crystallographic R value
               D~min~   = maximum resolution
               C        = completeness of data

               Ref: Cruickshank, D. W. J. (1999). Acta Cryst. D55, 583-601.

                    Murshudov, G. N. & Dodson,
                    E. J. (1997). Simplified error estimation a la
                    Cruickshank in macromolecular crystallography.
                    CCP4 Newsletter on Protein Crystallography, No. 33,
                    January 1997, pp. 31-39.

                   http://www.ccp4.ac.uk/newsletters/newsletter33/murshudov.html

_refine.overall_SU_R_free.name: _refine.overall_SU_R_free
_refine.overall_SU_R_free.description:
The overall standard uncertainty (estimated standard deviation)
               of the displacement parameters based on the free R value.

               The overall standard uncertainty (sigma~B~) gives an idea
               of the uncertainty in the B values of averagely defined
               atoms (atoms with B values equal to the average B value).

                                      N~a~
               (sigma~B~)^2^ = 0.65 ---------- (R~free~)^2^ (D~min~)^2^ C^-2/3^
                                    (N~o~-N~p~)


               N~a~     = number of atoms included in refinement
               N~o~     = number of observations
               N~p~     = number of parameters refined
               R~free~  = conventional free crystallographic R value calculated
                        using reflections not included in refinement
               D~min~   = maximum resolution
               C        = completeness of data

               Ref: Cruickshank, D. W. J. (1999). Acta Cryst. D55, 583-601.

                    Murshudov, G. N. & Dodson,
                    E. J. (1997). Simplified error estimation a la
                    Cruickshank in macromolecular crystallography.
                    CCP4 Newsletter on Protein Crystallography, No. 33,
                    January 1997, pp. 31-39.

                   http://www.ccp4.ac.uk/newsletters/newsletter33/murshudov.html

_refine.overall_FOM_free_R_set.name: _refine.overall_FOM_free_R_set
_refine.overall_FOM_free_R_set.description:
Average figure of merit of phases of reflections not included
               in the refinement.

               This value is derived from the likelihood function.

               FOM           = I~1~(X)/I~0~(X)

               I~0~, I~1~     = zero- and first-order modified Bessel functions
                               of the first kind
               X              = sigma~A~ |E~o~| |E~c~|/SIGMA
               E~o~, E~c~     = normalized observed and calculated structure
                               factors
               sigma~A~       = <cos 2 pi s delta~x~> SQRT(Sigma~P~/Sigma~N~)
                               estimated using maximum likelihood
               Sigma~P~       = sum~{atoms in model}~ f^2^
               Sigma~N~       = sum~{atoms in crystal}~ f^2^
               f              = form factor of atoms
               delta~x~       = expected error
               SIGMA          = (sigma~{E;exp}~)^2^ + epsilon [1-(sigma~A~)^2^]
               sigma~{E;exp}~ = uncertainties of normalized observed
                               structure factors
               epsilon       = multiplicity of the diffracting plane

               Ref: Murshudov, G. N., Vagin, A. A. & Dodson, E. J. (1997).
                    Acta Cryst. D53, 240-255.

_refine.overall_FOM_work_R_set.name: _refine.overall_FOM_work_R_set
_refine.overall_FOM_work_R_set.description:
Average figure of merit of phases of reflections included in
               the refinement.

               This value is derived from the likelihood function.

               FOM           = I~1~(X)/I~0~(X)

               I~0~, I~1~     = zero- and first-order modified Bessel functions
                               of the first kind
               X              = sigma~A~ |E~o~| |E~c~|/SIGMA
               E~o~, E~c~     = normalized observed and calculated structure
                               factors
               sigma~A~       = <cos 2 pi s delta~x~> SQRT(Sigma~P~/Sigma~N~)
                               estimated using maximum likelihood
               Sigma~P~       = sum~{atoms in model}~ f^2^
               Sigma~N~       = sum~{atoms in crystal}~ f^2^
               f              = form factor of atoms
               delta~x~       = expected error
               SIGMA          = (sigma~{E;exp}~)^2^ + epsilon [1-(sigma~A~)^2^]
               sigma~{E;exp}~ = uncertainties of normalized observed
                               structure factors
               epsilon       = multiplicity of the diffracting plane

               Ref: Murshudov, G. N., Vagin, A. A. & Dodson, E. J. (1997).
                    Acta Cryst. D53, 240-255.

_refln.pdbx_r_free_flag.name: _refln.pdbx_r_free_flag
_refln.pdbx_r_free_flag.description:
The R-free flag originally assigned to the reflection.  The convention used for
               labeling the work and test sets differs depending on choice of data processing
               software and refinement program.

_reflns.pdbx_CC_half.name: _reflns.pdbx_CC_half
_reflns.pdbx_CC_half.description:
The Pearson's correlation coefficient expressed as a decimal value
              between the average intensities from randomly selected
              half-datasets.

	      Ref: Karplus & Diederichs (2012), Science 336, 1030-33

_reflns.pdbx_CC_star.name: _reflns.pdbx_CC_star
_reflns.pdbx_CC_star.description:
Estimates the value of CC_true, the true correlation coefficient between
              the average intensities from randomly selected half-datasets.

              CC_star = sqrt(2*CC_half/(1+CC_half)), where both CC_star and CC_half (CC1/2)

	      Ref: Karplus & Diederichs (2012), Science 336, 1030-33

_reflns.pdbx_R_split.name: _reflns.pdbx_R_split
_reflns.pdbx_R_split.description:
R split measures the agreement between the sets of intensities created by merging
              odd- and even-numbered images  from the overall data.

	      Ref: T. A. White, R. A. Kirian, A. V. Martin, A. Aquila, K. Nass, A. Barty
              and H. N. Chapman (2012), J. Appl. Cryst. 45, 335-341

_reflns_shell.pdbx_CC_half.name: _reflns_shell.pdbx_CC_half
_reflns_shell.pdbx_CC_half.description:
The Pearson's correlation coefficient expressed as a decimal value
              between the average intensities from randomly selected
              half-datasets within the resolution shell.

	      Ref: Karplus & Diederichs (2012), Science 336, 1030-33

_reflns_shell.pdbx_CC_star.name: _reflns_shell.pdbx_CC_star
_reflns_shell.pdbx_CC_star.description:
Estimates the value of CC_true, the true correlation coefficient
              between the average intensities from randomly selected half-datasets
              within the resolution shell.

              CC_star = sqrt(2*CC_half/(1+CC_half))

	      Ref: Karplus & Diederichs (2012), Science 336, 1030-33

_reflns_shell.pdbx_R_split.name: _reflns_shell.pdbx_R_split
_reflns_shell.pdbx_R_split.description:
R split measures the agreement between the sets of intensities created by merging
              odd- and even-numbered images from the data within the resolution shell.

	      Ref: T. A. White, R. A. Kirian, A. V. Martin, A. Aquila, K. Nass,
	      A. Barty and H. N. Chapman (2012), J. Appl. Cryst. 45, 335-341

_em_detector.detective_quantum_efficienc.name: _em_detector.detective_quantum_efficiency
_em_detector.detective_quantum_efficienc.description:
The detective_quantum_efficiency (DQE)is defined as the
     square of the signal-to-noise ratio in the recording device
     divided by the square of the signal-to-ratio in the electron beam:
            (SIGNAL/NOISE)2 recording device
   DQE =    -------------------------------
            (SIGNAL/NOISE)2 electron beam
   A DQE value of 1 indicates a perfect recorder. "DQE = 0.25" menas
   that the signal-to-noise ratio is reduced by half in the
   recording step.
        (0.5)**2
  DQE = --------- = 0.25.
         (1.0)**2

_em_3d_reconstruction.resolution_method.name: _em_3d_reconstruction.resolution_method
_em_3d_reconstruction.resolution_method.description:
The  method used to determine the final resolution
     of the 3d reconstruction.
     The Fourier Shell Correlation criterion as a measure of
     resolution is based on the concept of splitting the (2D)
     data set into two halves; averaging each and comparing them
     using the Fourier Ring Correlation (FRC) technique.

_em_3d_reconstruction.magnification_cali.name: _em_3d_reconstruction.magnification_calibration
_em_3d_reconstruction.magnification_cali.description: "    The magnification calibration method for the 3d reconstruction."

_em_3d_reconstruction.ctf_correction_met.name: _em_3d_reconstruction.ctf_correction_method
_em_3d_reconstruction.ctf_correction_met.description:
The  CTF-correction method.
     The Contrast Transfer Function CTF compensation for low contrast
     specimens (e.g. frozen-hydrated), for which phase contrast is the only
     significant mechanism, then higher defocus levels must be used to
     achieve any significant transfer, and several images at different
     focus levels must be combined to complete the information lost from
     the transfer gaps of any one image.  The CTF correction can be applied
     to each extracted particle separately or to the whole micrograph after
     digitisation. The simplest level of compensation is to reverse phases
     at the negative lobes of the CTF.
examples: "    The volumes were CTF-corrected in defocus groups, with an average of approximately 999 individual images per group"

_em_depui.depositor_hold_instructions.name: _em_depui.depositor_hold_instructions
_em_depui.depositor_hold_instructions.description:
Choose the manner in which you would like the map and associated files (half
maps, additional maps, masks, FSC curves, structure factors, layer lines, and
images) to be released.  Release of these files can be delayed either until
publication of the associated primary citation or until one year after completion
of the deposition. It is the responsibility of the depositor to notify the EMDB when
the primary citation has been published. Please note that map-associated
experimental information and metadata (header data) are made available to the
public when an entry is placed on hold.

_em_depui.macromolecule_description.name: _em_depui.macromolecule_description
_em_depui.macromolecule_description.description: " Indicates whether the authors are providing a macromolecule level description of their sample"

_em_depui.obsolete_instructions.name: _em_depui.obsolete_instructions
_em_depui.obsolete_instructions.description:
Instruction for annotators on why a previous released entry should be obsoleted.
   Example of valid request:
   * Author wants to replace a map that has already been released, following discovery of a processing error
   Examples of invalid requests:
   * Someone other than the author wants to obsolete a map.
   * Legal conflict of interest
   Obsoletion is not required for the following actions:
   * to change released metadata info.
   * to update the map of an HPUB entry.
   * to change the hand of a released map.
   * to deposit an improved version of a released map.

_em_virus_shell.triangulation.name: _em_virus_shell.triangulation
_em_virus_shell.triangulation.description:
The triangulation number, T, describes the organization of subunits within an
icosahedron. T is defined as T= h^2 + h*k + k^2, where h and k are positive
integers that define the position of the five-fold vertex on the original
hexagonal net.

  was then blotted, air dried, and frozen in LN2.

_em_2d_crystal_entity.space_group_name_H.name: _em_2d_crystal_entity.space_group_name_H-M
_em_2d_crystal_entity.space_group_name_H.description:
There are 17 plane groups classified as oblique, rectangular, square, and hexagonal.
      To describe the symmetry of 2D crystals of biological molecules,
      plane groups are expanded to equivalent noncentrosymmetric space groups.
      The 2D crystal plane corresponds to the 'ab' plane of the space group.

      Enumerated space group descriptions include the plane group number in parentheses,
      the H-M plane group symbol, and the plane group class.

_em_map.data_type.name: _em_map.data_type
_em_map.data_type.description:
The map data_type describes the data structure of the map voxels.
    (CCP4 map header word 4 MODE)
    EMDB currently holds MODE=0,1,and 2 maps; the majority are MODE=2.
    MAPS with MODES other than 2 and 0 may not work in CCP4 programs.
    MODE = 0: 8 bits, density stored as a signed byte (-128 to 127, ISO/IEC 10967)
    MODE = 1: 16 bits, density stored as a signed integer (-32768 to 32767, ISO/IEC 10967)
    MODE = 2: 32 bits, density stored as a floating point number (IEEE 754)

enumeration.value: "Image stored as floating point number (4 bytes)"

_pdbx_chem_comp_depositor_info.formula.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_depositor_info.formula
_pdbx_chem_comp_depositor_info.formula.description:
The formula for the chemical component. Formulae are written
               according to the following rules:

               (1) Only recognized element symbols may be used.

               (2) Each element symbol is followed by a 'count' number. A count
                  of '1' may be omitted.

               (3) A space or parenthesis must separate each cluster of
                  (element symbol + count), but in general parentheses are
                  not used.

               (4) The order of elements depends on whether carbon is
                  present or not. If carbon is present, the order should be:
                  C, then H, then the other elements in alphabetical order
                  of their symbol. If carbon is not present, the elements
                  are listed purely in alphabetic order of their symbol. This
                  is the 'Hill' system used by Chemical Abstracts.

_pdbx_chem_comp_depositor_info.type.description: "              This data item contains the chemical component type."

_pdbx_struct_ref_seq_depositor_info.db_s.name: _pdbx_struct_ref_seq_depositor_info.db_seq_one_letter_code
_pdbx_struct_ref_seq_depositor_info.db_s.description:
Database chemical sequence expressed as string of one-letter
               residue codes.
examples:
A  for alanine or adenine
B  for ambiguous asparagine/aspartic-acid
R  for arginine
N  for asparagine
D  for aspartic-acid
C  for cysteine or cystine or cytosine
Q  for glutamine
E  for glutamic-acid
Z  for ambiguous glutamine/glutamic acid
G  for glycine or guanine
H  for histidine
I  for isoleucine
L  for leucine
K  for lysine
M  for methionine
F  for phenylalanine
P  for proline
S  for serine
T  for threonine or thymine
W  for tryptophan
Y  for tyrosine
V  for valine
U  for uracil
O  for water
X  for other

_pdbx_struct_assembly_gen_depositor_info.name: _pdbx_struct_assembly_gen_depositor_info.oper_expression
_pdbx_struct_assembly_gen_depositor_info.description:
Identifies the operation of collection of operations
               from category PDBX_STRUCT_OPER_LIST.

               Operation expressions may have the forms:

                (1)        the single operation 1
                (1,2,5)    the operations 1, 2, 5
                (1-4)      the operations 1,2,3 and 4
                (1,2)(3,4) the combinations of operations
                           3 and 4 followed by 1 and 2 (i.e.
                           the cartesian product of parenthetical
                           groups applied from right to left)

_pdbx_chem_comp_instance_depositor_info..name: _pdbx_chem_comp_instance_depositor_info.formula
_pdbx_chem_comp_instance_depositor_info..description:
The observed formula for the chemical component in the deposited coordinates.
               Formulae are written according to the following rules:

               (1) Only recognized element symbols may be used.

               (2) Each element symbol is followed by a 'count' number. A count
                  of '1' may be omitted.

               (3) A space or parenthesis must separate each cluster of
                  (element symbol + count), but in general parentheses are
                  not used.

               (4) The order of elements depends on whether carbon is
                  present or not. If carbon is present, the order should be:
                  C, then H, then the other elements in alphabetical order
                  of their symbol. If carbon is not present, the elements
                  are listed purely in alphabetic order of their symbol. This
                  is the 'Hill' system used by Chemical Abstracts.

_atom_type.pdbx_scat_Cromer_Mann_a5.description:
Scattering-factor coefficient a5, used to calculate electron
               elastic atomic scattering factors for the defined atom type.

               Electron Elastic Scattering Factors Ref:
                   International Tables for X-ray Crystallography (2006).
                   Vol. C, Table 4.3.2.2, pp. 282-283.

               Cromer_Mann equation Ref:
                   International Tables for X-ray Crystallography (1974).
                   Vol. IV, Table 2.2B
                or: International Tables for Crystallography  (2004). Vol. C,
                    Tables 6.1.1.4 and 6.1.1.5.

_refine.pdbx_average_fsc_overall.name: _refine.pdbx_average_fsc_overall
_refine.pdbx_average_fsc_overall.description:
Overall average Fourier Shell Correlation (avgFSC) between model and
     observed structure factors for all reflections.

     The average FSC is a measure of the agreement between observed
     and calculated structure factors.

                sum(N~i~ FSC~i~)
     avgFSC   = ----------------
                sum(N~i~)


     N~i~     = the number of all reflections in the resolution shell i
     FSC~i~   = FSC for all reflections in the i-th resolution shell calculated as:

               (sum(|F~o~| |F~c~| fom cos(phi~c~-phi~o~)))
     FSC~i~  = -------------------------------------------
               (sum(|F~o~|^2^) (sum(|F~c~|^2^)))^1/2^

     |F~o~|   = amplitude of observed structure factor
     |F~c~|   = amplitude of calculated structure factor
     phi~o~   = phase of observed structure factor
     phi~c~   = phase of calculated structure factor
     fom      = figure of merit of the experimental phases.

     Summation of FSC~i~ is carried over all reflections in the resolution shell.

     Summation of avgFSC is carried over all resolution shells.


     Ref:  Rosenthal P.B., Henderson R.
           "Optimal determination of particle orientation, absolute hand,
           and contrast loss in single-particle electron cryomicroscopy.
           Journal of Molecular Biology. 2003;333(4):721-745, equation (A6).

_refine.pdbx_average_fsc_work.name: _refine.pdbx_average_fsc_work
_refine.pdbx_average_fsc_work.description:
Average Fourier Shell Correlation (avgFSC) between model and
     observed structure factors for reflections included in refinement.

     The average FSC is a measure of the agreement between observed
     and calculated structure factors.

                      sum(N~i~ FSC~work-i~)
     avgFSC~work~   = ---------------------
                      sum(N~i~)


     N~i~          = the number of working reflections in the resolution shell i
     FSC~work-i~   = FSC for working reflections in the i-th resolution shell calculated as:

                    (sum(|F~o~| |F~c~| fom cos(phi~c~-phi~o~)))
     FSC~work-i~  = -------------------------------------------
                    (sum(|F~o~|^2^) (sum(|F~c~|^2^)))^1/2^

     |F~o~|   = amplitude of observed structure factor
     |F~c~|   = amplitude of calculated structure factor
     phi~o~   = phase of observed structure factor
     phi~c~   = phase of calculated structure factor
     fom      = figure of merit of the experimental phases.

     Summation of FSC~work-i~ is carried over all working reflections in the resolution shell.

     Summation of avgFSC~work~ is carried over all resolution shells.


     Ref:  Rosenthal P.B., Henderson R.
           "Optimal determination of particle orientation, absolute hand,
           and contrast loss in single-particle electron cryomicroscopy.
           Journal of Molecular Biology. 2003;333(4):721-745, equation (A6).

_refine.pdbx_average_fsc_free.name: _refine.pdbx_average_fsc_free
_refine.pdbx_average_fsc_free.description:
Average Fourier Shell Correlation (avgFSC) between model and
     observed structure factors for reflections not included in refinement.

     The average FSC is a measure of the agreement between observed
     and calculated structure factors.

                      sum(N~i~ FSC~free-i~)
     avgFSC~free~   = ---------------------
                      sum(N~i~)


     N~i~          = the number of free reflections in the resolution shell i
     FSC~free-i~   = FSC for free reflections in the i-th resolution shell calculated as:

                    (sum(|F~o~| |F~c~| fom cos(phi~c~-phi~o~)))
     FSC~free-i~  = -------------------------------------------
                    (sum(|F~o~|^2^) (sum(|F~c~|^2^)))^1/2^

     |F~o~|   = amplitude of observed structure factor
     |F~c~|   = amplitude of calculated structure factor
     phi~o~   = phase of observed structure factor
     phi~c~   = phase of calculated structure factor
     fom      = figure of merit of the experimental phases.

     Summation of FSC~free-i~ is carried over all free reflections in the resolution shell.

     Summation of avgFSC~free~ is carried over all resolution shells.


     Ref:  Rosenthal P.B., Henderson R.
           "Optimal determination of particle orientation, absolute hand,
           and contrast loss in single-particle electron cryomicroscopy.
           Journal of Molecular Biology. 2003;333(4):721-745, equation (A6).

_refine_ls_shell.pdbx_fsc_work.name: _refine_ls_shell.pdbx_fsc_work
_refine_ls_shell.pdbx_fsc_work.description:
Fourier Shell Correlation (FSC) between model and
     observed structure factors for reflections included in refinement.

     FSC is a measure of the agreement between observed
     and calculated structure factors as complex numbers.

                  (sum(|F~o~| |F~c~| fom cos(phi~c~-phi~o~)))
     FSC~work~  = --------------------------------------
                  (sum(|F~o~|^2^) (sum(|F~c~|^2^)))^1/2^

     |F~o~|   = amplitude of observed structure factor
     |F~c~|   = amplitude of calculated structure factor
     phi~o~   = phase of observed structure factor
     phi~c~   = phase of calculated structure factor
     fom      = figure of merit of the experimental phases.

     Summation is carried over all working reflections in the resolution shell.

     Ref:  Rosenthal P.B., Henderson R.
           "Optimal determination of particle orientation, absolute hand,
           and contrast loss in single-particle electron cryomicroscopy.
           Journal of Molecular Biology. 2003;333(4):721-745, equation (A6).

_refine_ls_shell.pdbx_fsc_free.name: _refine_ls_shell.pdbx_fsc_free
_refine_ls_shell.pdbx_fsc_free.description:
Fourier Shell Correlation (FSC) between model and
     observed structure factors for reflections not included in refinement.

     FSC is a measure of the agreement between observed
     and calculated structure factors as complex numbers.

                  (sum(|F~o~| |F~c~| fom cos(phi~c~-phi~o~)))
     FSC~free~  = --------------------------------------
                  (sum(|F~o~|^2^) (sum(|F~c~|^2^)))^1/2^

     |F~o~|   = amplitude of observed structure factor
     |F~c~|   = amplitude of calculated structure factor
     phi~o~   = phase of observed structure factor
     phi~c~   = phase of calculated structure factor
     fom      = figure of merit of the experimental phases.

     Summation is carried over all free reflections in the resolution shell.

     Ref:  Rosenthal P.B., Henderson R.
           "Optimal determination of particle orientation, absolute hand,
           and contrast loss in single-particle electron cryomicroscopy.
           Journal of Molecular Biology. 2003;333(4):721-745, equation (A6).

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_direction_2.description: "The crystal size along the second measured direction in millimeters."

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_direction_3.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_crystal_direction_3

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_method.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_method
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_method.description:
How parameters derived from the spot shape (such as mosaic block
               size and rotation, beam divergence, and crossfire) and their
	       errors were estimated. See the related items section.

	       This can be a written description or a citation to a specific
	       software package that determined these parameters.

	       Note, these parameters are considered derived terms from the
	       data, as opposed to measured terms from the instrument (such
	       as diffrn_radiation.div_x_source, a term similar to
	       pdbx_crystal_alignment.crossfire_x).

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_block_size.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_block_size
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_block_size.description:
Isotropic and resolution-independent term representing the average size of
    mosaic domains in the crystal specified in Angstroms.  Larger size indicates
    better ordered crystals.

_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_block_size_es.name: _exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_block_size_esd
_exptl_crystal.pdbx_mosaic_block_size_es.description: "    The uncertainty in the mosaic block size estimate for the crystal."

Hydrogen bonds between peptide chains follow the Rich and Crick

_refine.pdbx_overall_ESU_B.name: _refine.pdbx_overall_ESU_B
_refine.pdbx_overall_ESU_B.description:
Overall estimated standard uncertainties of thermal parameters
               based on Maximum likelihood residual.

               Overall ESU gives an idea about uncertainties of B-values of
               averagely defined atoms (atoms with B-values equal to average
               B-value)

                                          N_a
  (sigma_B)^2  = 8 ----------------------------------------------
                   sum~i~ {(1/Sigma - (E_o)^2 (1-m^2)(SUM_AS)s^4}

               SUM_AS         = (sigma_A)^2/Sigma^2)
               N_a            = number of atoms
               Sigma          = (sigma_{E;exp})^2 + epsilon (1-{sigma_A)^2)
               E_o            = normalized structure factors
               sigma_{E;exp}  = experimental uncertainties of normalized
                                structure factors
               sigma_A        = <cos 2 pi s delta_x> SQRT(Sigma_P/Sigma_N)
                                estimated using maximum likelihood
               Sigma_P        = sum_{atoms in model} f^2
               Sigma_N        = sum_{atoms in crystal} f^2
               f              = is form factor of atoms
               delta_x        = expected error
               m              = is figure of merit of phases of reflection
                                included in summation delta_x expected error
               s              = reciprocal space vector
               epsilon        = multiplicity of diffracting plane

               summation is over all reflections included in refinement

               Reference for sigma_A estimation:

               "Refinement of Macromolecular Structures by the
               Maximum-Likelihood Method:"  G.N. Murshudov, A.A.Vagin and
               E.J.Dodson,(1997) Acta Crystallogr. D53, 240-255

               Reference for ESU_ML estimation:

               "Simplified error estimation a la Cruickshank in macromolecular
               crystallography",  Murshudov G.N. & Dodson E.J.  in the "CCP4
               Newsletter on protein crystallography" Number 33 ed. M.Winn

_refine.pdbx_overall_ESU_ML.name: _refine.pdbx_overall_ESU_ML
_refine.pdbx_overall_ESU_ML.description:
Overall estimated standard uncertainties of positional
               parameters based on Maximum likelihood residual.

               Overall ESU gives an idea about uncertainties in the position
               of averagely defined atoms (atoms with B-values equal to
               average B-value)

                 3                         N_a
  (sigma_X)^2  = -----------------------------------------------------
                 8 pi^2 sum~i~ {(1/Sigma - (E_o)^2 (1-m^2)(SUM_AS)s^2}

               SUM_AS         = (sigma_A)^2/Sigma^2)
               N_a            = number of atoms
               Sigma          = (sigma_{E;exp})^2 + epsilon (1-{sigma_A)^2)
               E_o            = normalized structure factors

               sigma_{E;exp}  = experimental uncertainties of normalized
                                structure factors
               sigma_A        = <cos 2 pi s delta_x> SQRT(Sigma_P/Sigma_N)
                                estimated using maximum likelihood
               Sigma_P        = sum_{atoms in model} f^2
               Sigma_N        = sum_{atoms in crystal} f^2
               f              = is formfactor of atoms
               delta_x        = expected error
               m              = is figure of merit of phases of reflection
                                included in summation delta_x expected error
               s              = reciprocal space vector
               epsilon        = multiplicity of diffracting plane

               summation is over all reflections included in refinement

               Reference for sigma_A estimation:

               "Refinement of Macromolecular Structures by the
               Maximum-Likelihood Method:" G.N. Murshudov, A.A.Vagin and
               E.J.Dodson,(1997)  Acta Crystallogr. D53, 240-255

               Reference for ESU_ML estimation:

               Simplified error estimation a la Cruickshank in macromolecular
               crystallograpy  Murshudov G.N. & Dodson E.J.  in the "CCP4
               Newsletter on protein crystallography" Number 33 ed. M.Winn

_pdbx_dcc_density.Z_score_L_test.description:
Multivariate Z score for the L-test calculated by Xtriage using
	acentric reflections. It is a quality measure of the given spread
	in intensities. Good to reasonable data are expected to have a
	Z score lower than 3.5.

_pdbx_dcc_density.twin_type.name: _pdbx_dcc_density.twin_type
_pdbx_dcc_density.twin_type.description:
Type of twin (merohedral,Pseudo-merohedral or non-merohedral)
	determined by Xtriage.

_pdbx_dcc_density.twin_operator_xtriage.name: _pdbx_dcc_density.twin_operator_xtriage
_pdbx_dcc_density.twin_operator_xtriage.description: "       A list of the twin operators determined by Xtriage."

_pdbx_dcc_density.twin_fraction_xtriage.name: _pdbx_dcc_density.twin_fraction_xtriage
_pdbx_dcc_density.twin_fraction_xtriage.description: "       The twin fraction determined by Xtriage."

_pdbx_dcc_density.twin_Rfactor.name: _pdbx_dcc_density.twin_Rfactor
_pdbx_dcc_density.twin_Rfactor.description:

	data. (Smaller values indicate twins).

_pdbx_dcc_density.I_over_sigI_resh.name: _pdbx_dcc_density.I_over_sigI_resh

_pdbx_dcc_density.I_over_sigI_diff.name: _pdbx_dcc_density.I_over_sigI_diff
_pdbx_dcc_density.I_over_sigI_diff.description:

_pdbx_dcc_density.I_over_sigI_mean.name: _pdbx_dcc_density.I_over_sigI_mean
_pdbx_dcc_density.I_over_sigI_mean.description: "       The overall mean value of <I/SigmaI> for the reflections."

_pdbx_dcc_density.ice_ring.name: _pdbx_dcc_density.ice_ring
_pdbx_dcc_density.ice_ring.description:

_pdbx_dcc_density.Z-score.name: _pdbx_dcc_density.Z-score
_pdbx_dcc_density.Z-score.description:
Z-scores are computed (by Xtriage) on the basis of a Bernoulli
	model assuming independence of weak reflections with respect to
	anisotropy.

_pdbx_dcc_density.prob_peak_value.name: _pdbx_dcc_density.prob_peak_value
_pdbx_dcc_density.prob_peak_value.description:
The probability to tell the existence of translational pseudo
	symmetry. p_values smaller than 0.05 (1e-3) might indicate weak
	(strong) translational pseudo symmetry (by Xtriage).

_pdbx_dcc_density.translational_pseudo_s.description:

_pdbx_dcc_map.model_id.description: "       The component model identifier for this analysis."

_pdbx_dcc_map.auth_comp_id.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.auth_comp_id
_pdbx_dcc_map.auth_comp_id.description: "       The author provided compound ID (residue name)."

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC.description:
The Real Space electron density Correlation Coefficient for the
	residue. It is defined as
	RSCC=(<xy>-<x><y>)/[sqrt(<x**2>-<x>**2)*sqrt(<y**2>-<y>**2)]
	where x is the observed density from the (2mFo-DFc) map and y is the
	calculated density from the Fc map

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSR.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSR
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSR.description:
The Real space Rfactor (RSR) for the residue. It is defined as
	RSR=sum(|x-y|/x+y|) which is the summation over all grid points
	around the residue, where x is the observed density from the
	(2mFo-DFc) map and y is the calculated density from the Fc map.

_pdbx_dcc_map.weighted_RSR.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.weighted_RSR
_pdbx_dcc_map.weighted_RSR.description: "       The weighted Real Space Rfactor (defined as RSR/RSCC) for the residue."

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ.description:
Zscore of the Real Space Rfactor (RSRZ) for the residue. It is defined
	as (RSR-<RSR>)/sigma_RSR, where <RSR> and sigma_RSR are for the whole map,
	RSR is for the residue.

_pdbx_dcc_map.weighted_RSRZ.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.weighted_RSRZ
_pdbx_dcc_map.weighted_RSRZ.description:
Zscore of weighted Real Space Rfactor for the residue. It is defined as
	(wRSR-<wRSR>)/sigma_wRSR,  where <wRSR> and sigma_wRSR are for the whole map,
	wRSR is for the residue.

_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean
_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean.description:
The occupancy weighted average isotropic B factors for the residue.
	It is defined as (SUM B*Q)/(SUM Q), where B is the isotropic B factor
	for each atom and Q is the occupancy.

_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean
_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean.description: "       The averaged occupancy for the residue."

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_main_chain.description: "       The Real Space electron density Correlation Coefficient for the main chain atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSR_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSR_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSR_main_chain.description: "       The Real space Rfactor (RSR) for the main chain atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSR_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.wRSR_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSR_main_chain.description: "       The weighted Real Space Rfactor (RSR/RSCC) for the main chain atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSRZ_main_chain.description: "       Zscore of the Real Space Rfactor (RSRZ) for the main chain atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSRZ_main_chain.description: "       Zscore of weighted Real Space Rfactor (RSR/RSCC) for the main chain atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_main_chain.description: "       The occupancy weighted average isotropic B factors for the main chain atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_main_chain.description: "       The averaged occupancy forthe main chain atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSCC_side_chain

_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSR_side_chain.description: "       The weighted Real Space Rfactor (RSR/RSCC) for the side chain atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSRZ_phosphate_group.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.wRSRZ_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.wRSRZ_phosphate_group.description: "       Zscore of weighted Real Space Rfactor (RSR/RSCC) for the phosphate atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_phosphate_group.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.Biso_mean_phosphate_group.description: "       The occupancy weighted average isotropic B factors for the phosphate atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_phosphate_g.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.occupancy_mean_phosphate_g.description: "       The averaged occupancy forthe phosphate atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.shift.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.shift
_pdbx_dcc_map.shift.description:
Defined in sfcheck (displacement/sigma_of_displacement).
	Displacement of atoms from electron density is estimated from the
	difference (Fobs - Fcal) map. The displacement vector is the ratio
	of the gradient of difference density to the curvature. The amplitude
	of the displacement vector is an indicator of the positional error.

_pdbx_dcc_map.shift_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.shift_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.shift_main_chain.description: "       The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.density_shift, but it is for main chain."

_pdbx_dcc_map.shift_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.shift_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.shift_side_chain.description: "       The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.density_shift, but it is for side chain."

_pdbx_dcc_map.density_connectivity.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.density_connectivity
_pdbx_dcc_map.density_connectivity.description:
Defined in sfcheck. It is the product of the (2Fobs-Fcal) electron
	density values for the backbone atoms N, CA and C, and in the case of
	RNA/DNA, for P, O5', C5', C3', O3'. Low values of this index indicate
	breaks in the backbone electron density which may be due to flexibility
	of the chain or incorrect tracing.

_pdbx_dcc_map.density_index_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.density_index_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.density_index_main_chain.description:
Defined in sfcheck. It is the mean density of the main chain atoms
	divided by the mean density of the map.

_pdbx_dcc_map.density_index_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.density_index_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.density_index_side_chain.description:
Defined in sfcheck. It is the mean density of the side chain atoms
	divided by the mean density of the map.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD.description:
The real space difference density Z score
	(defined as Delta_rho/sigma(Delta_rho)) from Tickle (2012). It is
	related to the model accuracy. RSZD>3, significant extra positive
	density around the residue; RSZD<-3, significant extra negative
	density around the residue.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO.description:
The real space observed density Z score (defined as <rho_obs>/sigma(Delta_rho))
	from Tickle (2012). It is related to the model precision (B factors).
	Small or large value means weak or strong density for the residue.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_Zscore.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_Zscore
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_Zscore.description: "       The Zscore of RSZO (or the Zscore of <rho_obs>/sigma(Delta_rho))."

_pdbx_dcc_map.LLDF.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.LLDF
_pdbx_dcc_map.LLDF.description:
It is the Ligand Local Density Function defined as the
	(<RsR>_local - RsR_Ligand)/sigma_RsR, where <RsR>_local is the
	mean RSR of polymer residues in 5 angstroms (including the
	crystallographic symmetry) surrounding the ligand, and sigma_RsR
	is sigma value calculated from the surrounding residues.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_main_chain.description: "       The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD, use for the main chain atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_main_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_main_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_main_chain.description: "       The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO, use for the main chain atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_side_chain.description: "       The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD, use for the side chain atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_side_chain.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_side_chain
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_side_chain.description: "       The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO, use for the side chain atoms."

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_phosphate_group.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD_phosphate_group.description:
The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZD, use for the phosphate atoms of
        nucleic acids.

_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_phosphate_group.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_phosphate_group
_pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO_phosphate_group.description:
The same as _pdbx_dcc_map.RSZO, use for the phosphate atoms of
        nucleic acids.

_pdbx_dcc_map.quality_indicator.name: _pdbx_dcc_map.quality_indicator
_pdbx_dcc_map.quality_indicator.description:
The value indicates any problems with this group of atoms. If given
	as 'd', it means there is problem of local model accuracy. If given
	as 'w', it means weak density (there is problem of local model
        precision). If given as 'dw', both then problems exist.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_fit.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_fit
_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_high_fit.description:
The highest resolution limit used for rigid body
        refinement after molecular replacement (MR) solution.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_low_fit.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_low_fit
_pdbx_phasing_MR.d_res_low_fit.description:
The lowest resolution limit used for rigid body
        refinement after molecular replacement (MR) solution.

_pdbx_phasing_MR.zscore_rotation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.zscore_rotation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.zscore_rotation.description:
It is computed by comparing the LLG values from the rotation
        search with LLG values for a set of random rotations. The mean and
        the RMS deviation from the mean are computed from the random set,
        then the Z-score for a search peak is defined as its LLG minus the
        mean, all divided by the RMS deviation. Read, R.J. (2001). "Pushing
        the boundaries of molecular replacement with maximum likelihood."
        Acta Cryst. D57, 1373-1382

_pdbx_phasing_MR.LL_gain_rotation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.LL_gain_rotation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.LL_gain_rotation.description:
The log-likelihood gain after rotation. Read, R.J. (2001). "Pushing
        the boundaries of molecular replacement with maximum likelihood."
        Acta Cryst. D57, 1373-1382

_pdbx_phasing_MR.zscore_translation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.zscore_translation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.zscore_translation.description:
It is computed by comparing the LLG values from the translation
        search with LLG values for a set of random translations. The mean
        and the RMS deviation from the mean are computed from the random set,
        then the Z-score for a search peak is defined as its LLG minus
        the mean, all divided by the RMS deviation. Read, R.J. (2001). "Pushing
        the boundaries of molecular replacement with maximum likelihood."
        Acta Cryst. D57, 1373-1382

_pdbx_phasing_MR.LL_gain_translation.name: _pdbx_phasing_MR.LL_gain_translation
_pdbx_phasing_MR.LL_gain_translation.description:
The log-likelihood gain after translation. Read, R.J. (2001). "Pushing
        the boundaries of molecular replacement with maximum likelihood."
         Acta Cryst. D57, 1373-1382

               crystallization process, the final temperature should be noted
               here and the protocol  involving multiple temperatures should be
               described in _exptl_crystal_grow.details.

_refine_ls_shell.R_factor_R_free.name: _refine_ls_shell.R_factor_R_free
_refine_ls_shell.R_factor_R_free.description:
Residual factor R for reflections that satisfy the resolution
               limits established by _refine_ls_shell.d_res_high and
               _refine_ls_shell.d_res_low and the observation limit
               established by _reflns.observed_criterion, and that were
               used as the test reflections (i.e. were excluded from the
               refinement) when the refinement included the calculation
               of a 'free' R factor. Details of how reflections were assigned
               to the working and test sets are given in _reflns.R_free_details.

                   sum|F~obs~ - F~calc~|
               R = ---------------------
                        sum|F~obs~|

               F~obs~  = the observed structure-factor amplitudes
               F~calc~ = the calculated structure-factor amplitudes

               sum is taken over the specified reflections

_reflns_shell.percent_possible_all.name: _reflns_shell.percent_possible_all
_reflns_shell.percent_possible_all.description:
The percentage of geometrically possible reflections represented
               by all reflections measured for this shell.

_reflns_shell.Rmerge_I_all.name: _reflns_shell.Rmerge_I_all
_reflns_shell.Rmerge_I_all.description:
The value of Rmerge(I) for all reflections in a given shell.

                           sum~i~(sum~j~|I~j~ - <I>|)
               Rmerge(I) = --------------------------
                               sum~i~(sum~j~<I>)

               I~j~ = the intensity of the jth observation of reflection i
               <I>  = the mean of the intensities of all observations of
                      reflection i

               sum~i~ is taken over all reflections
               sum~j~ is taken over all observations of each reflection

_reflns_shell.Rmerge_I_obs.name: _reflns_shell.Rmerge_I_obs
_reflns_shell.Rmerge_I_obs.description:
The value of Rmerge(I) for reflections classified as 'observed'
               (see _reflns.observed_criterion) in a given shell.

                           sum~i~(sum~j~|I~j~ - <I>|)
               Rmerge(I) = --------------------------
                               sum~i~(sum~j~<I>)

               I~j~ = the intensity of the jth observation of reflection i
               <I>  = the mean of the intensities of all observations of
                      reflection i

               sum~i~ is taken over all reflections
               sum~j~ is taken over all observations of each reflection

_reflns.pdbx_Rmerge_I_obs.description:
