_reflns.pdbx_orthogonalization_conventio.name: _reflns.pdbx_orthogonalization_convention
_reflns.pdbx_orthogonalization_conventio.description:
Description of orthogonalization convention used. The
              notation can make use of unit cell axes "a", "b" and "c"
              and the reciprocal unit cell axes "astar", "bstar" and
              "cstar". Upper case letters "X", "Y" and "Z" denote the
              orthogonal axes, while lower case "x" stands for "cross
              product".

enumeration.detail: "X along a, Y along (cstar x a), Z along cstar, with origins conincident"

_reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_ellipsoida.name: _reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_ellipsoidal
_reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_ellipsoida.description:
Completeness (as a percentage) of symmetry-unique data
               within the intersection of (1) a sphere (defined by the
               diffraction limits, _reflns.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns.d_resolution_low) and (2) the ellipsoid
               (described by __reflns.pdbx_aniso_diffraction_limit_*
               items), relative to all possible symmetry-unique
               reflections within that intersection.

_reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_spherical.name: _reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_spherical
_reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_spherical.description:
Completeness (as a percentage) of symmetry-unique data
               within the sphere defined by the diffraction limits
               (_reflns.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns.d_resolution_low) relative to all possible
               symmetry-unique reflections within that sphere.

               In the absence of an anisotropy description this is
               identical to _reflns.percent_possible_obs.

_reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_ellipsoida.name: _reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_ellipsoidal_anomalous
_reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_ellipsoida.description:
Completeness (as a percentage) of symmetry-unique
               anomalous difference data within the intersection of
               (1) a sphere (defined by the diffraction limits,
               _reflns.d_resolution_high and _reflns.d_resolution_low)
               and (2) the ellipsoid (described by
               __reflns.pdbx_aniso_diffraction_limit_* items),
               relative to all possible symmetry-unique anomalous
               difference data within that intersection.

_reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_spherical_.name: _reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_spherical_anomalous
_reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_spherical_.description:
Completeness (as a percentage) of symmetry-unique
               anomalous difference data within the sphere defined by
               the diffraction limits (_reflns.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns.d_resolution_low) relative to all possible
               symmetry-unique anomalous difference data within that
               sphere.

               In the absence of an anisotropy description this is
               identical to _reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_anomalous.

_reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_elli.name: _reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_ellipsoidal
_reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_elli.description:
Completeness (as a percentage) of symmetry-unique data
               within the intersection of (1) a spherical shell
               (defined by its diffraction limits,
               _reflns_shell.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns_shell.d_resolution_low) and (2) the ellipsoid
               (described by __reflns.pdbx_aniso_diffraction_limit_*
               items), relative to all possible symmetry-unique
               reflections within that intersection.

_reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_sphe.name: _reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_spherical
_reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_sphe.description:
Completeness (as a percentage) of symmetry-unique data
               within the spherical shell defined by its diffraction
               limits (_reflns_shell.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns_shell.d_resolution_low) relative to all
               possible symmetry-unique reflections within that shell.

               In the absence of an anisotropy description this is
               identical to _reflns_shell.percent_possible_all.

_reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_elli.name: _reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_ellipsoidal_anomalous
_reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_elli.description:
Completeness (as a percentage) of symmetry-unique
               anomalous difference data within the intersection of
               (1) a spherical shell (defined by its diffraction
               limits, _reflns_shell.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns_shell.d_resolution_low) and (2) the ellipsoid
               (described by __reflns.pdbx_aniso_diffraction_limit_*
               items), relative to all possible symmetry-unique
               anomalous difference data within that intersection.

_reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_sphe.name: _reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_spherical_anomalous
_reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_sphe.description:
Completeness (as a percentage) of symmetry-unique
               anomalous difference data within the spherical shell
               defined by its diffraction limits
               (_reflns_shell.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns_shell.d_resolution_low) relative to all
               possible symmetry-unique anomalous difference data
               within that shell.

               In the absence of an anisotropy description this is
               identical to _reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_anomalous.

_reflns.pdbx_redundancy_anomalous.name: _reflns.pdbx_redundancy_anomalous
_reflns.pdbx_redundancy_anomalous.description:
The overall redundancy of anomalous difference data
               within the sphere defined by the diffraction limits
               (_reflns.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns.d_resolution_low), i.e. data for which
               intensities for both instances of a Friedel pair are
               available for an acentric reflection.

_reflns.pdbx_CC_half_anomalous.name: _reflns.pdbx_CC_half_anomalous
_reflns.pdbx_CC_half_anomalous.description:
The overall correlation coefficient between two randomly
               chosen half-sets of anomalous intensity differences,
               I(+)-I(-) for anomalous data within the sphere defined
               by the diffraction limits (_reflns.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns.d_resolution_low), i.e. data for which
               intensities for both instances of a Friedel pair are
               available for an acentric reflection.

_reflns.pdbx_absDiff_over_sigma_anomalou.name: _reflns.pdbx_absDiff_over_sigma_anomalous
_reflns.pdbx_absDiff_over_sigma_anomalou.description:
The overall mean ratio of absolute anomalous intensity
               differences to their standard deviation within the
               sphere defined by the diffraction limits
               (_reflns.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns.d_resolution_low) and using data for which
               intensities for both instances of a Friedel pair are
               available for an acentric reflection.

                    |Dano|
                 -------------
                  sigma(Dano)

               with

                 Dano        = I(+) - I(-)
                 sigma(Dano) = sqrt( sigma(I(+))^2 + sigma(I(-))^2 )

_reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_anomalous.name: _reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_anomalous
_reflns.pdbx_percent_possible_anomalous.description:
Completeness (as a percentage) of symmetry-unique
               anomalous difference data within the sphere defined by
               the diffraction limits (_reflns.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns.d_resolution_low) relative to all possible
               symmetry-unique anomalous difference data within that
               sphere.

_reflns_shell.pdbx_redundancy_anomalous.name: _reflns_shell.pdbx_redundancy_anomalous
_reflns_shell.pdbx_redundancy_anomalous.description:
The redundancy of anomalous difference data within the
               spherical shell (defined by its diffraction limits
               _reflns_shell.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns_shell.d_resolution_low), i.e. data for which
               intensities for both instances of a Friedel pair are
               available for an acentric reflection.

_reflns_shell.pdbx_CC_half_anomalous.name: _reflns_shell.pdbx_CC_half_anomalous
_reflns_shell.pdbx_CC_half_anomalous.description:
The correlation coefficient within the spherical shell
               (defined by its diffraction limits
               _reflns_shell.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns_shell.d_resolution_low) between two randomly
               chosen half-sets of anomalous intensity differences,
               I(+)-I(-) for anomalous data, i.e. data for which
               intensities for both instances of a Friedel pair are
               available for an acentric reflection.

_reflns_shell.pdbx_absDiff_over_sigma_an.name: _reflns_shell.pdbx_absDiff_over_sigma_anomalous
_reflns_shell.pdbx_absDiff_over_sigma_an.description:
The mean ratio of absolute anomalous intensity
               differences to their standard deviation within the
               spherical shell (defined by its diffraction limits
               _reflns_shell.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns_shell.d_resolution_low).

                    |Dano|
                 -------------
                  sigma(Dano)

               with

                 Dano        = I(+) - I(-)
                 sigma(Dano) = sqrt( sigma(I(+))^2 + sigma(I(-))^2 )

_reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_anom.name: _reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_anomalous
_reflns_shell.pdbx_percent_possible_anom.description:
Completeness (as a percentage) of symmetry-unique
               anomalous difference data within the spherical shell
               defined by its diffraction limits
               (_reflns_shell.d_resolution_high and
               _reflns_shell.d_resolution_low) relative to all
               possible symmetry-unique anomalous difference data
               within that shell.

_reflns.pdbx_observed_signal_threshold.name: _reflns.pdbx_observed_signal_threshold
_reflns.pdbx_observed_signal_threshold.description:
The threshold value for _refln.pdbx_signal as used to
              define the status of an individual reflection according
              to the description in _refln.pdbx_signal_status.

_reflns.pdbx_signal_type.name: _reflns.pdbx_signal_type
_reflns.pdbx_signal_type.description:
Type of signal used for
              _reflns.pdbx_observed_signal_threshold and _refln.pdbx_signal

              In the enumeration details:

                 Imean is the inverse-variance weighted mean intensity of all
                 measurements for a given symmetry-unique reflection

                 Ihalf is the inverse-variance weighted mean intensity of a
                 random half-selection of all measurements for a
                 given symmetry-unique reflection

enumeration.detail: "Local weighted Pearson product-moment correlation coefficient of Ihalf/sigma(Imean) values"

_reflns.pdbx_signal_details.name: _reflns.pdbx_signal_details
_reflns.pdbx_signal_details.description:
Further details about the calculation of the values
              assigned to _refln.pdbx_signal

_reflns.pdbx_signal_software_id.name: _reflns.pdbx_signal_software_id
_reflns.pdbx_signal_software_id.description: "              The software used to calculate the values of _refln.pdbx_signal"

_pdbx_refln_signal_binning.ordinal.name: _pdbx_refln_signal_binning.ordinal
_pdbx_refln_signal_binning.ordinal.description:
The ordinal number of the bin, ordered from lowest signal
               to highest.

_pdbx_refln_signal_binning.upper_thresho.name: _pdbx_refln_signal_binning.upper_threshold
_pdbx_refln_signal_binning.upper_thresho.description: "              The upper (exclusive) threshold of the bin."

_refln.pdbx_signal.name: _refln.pdbx_signal
_refln.pdbx_signal.description:
The signal value for this reflection as defined by
               _reflns.pdbx_signal_type and _reflns.pdbx_signal_details
               as calculated by _reflns.pdbx_signal_software_id.

_refln.pdbx_signal_status.name: _refln.pdbx_signal_status
_refln.pdbx_signal_status.description:
The status of a reflection related to _refln.pdbx_signal.

               A measured reflection counts as observed if:
                  _refln.pdbx_signal >= _reflns.pdbx_observed_signal_threshold
               and unobserved if:
                  _refln.pdbx_signal <  _reflns.pdbx_observed_signal_threshold

               An unmeasured but observable reflection is one that has not
               been measured, but the data processing has determined that it
               would have been expected to be observed had it been measured.

               An unmeasured and unobservable reflection is one that the data
               processing has determined would not have been expected to be
               observed.

               In datasets in which _refln.pdbx_signal has been populated, a null
               (?) value for this item indicates an unmeasured reflection for
               which it is not known whether it is observable or not.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_x.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_x
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_x.description:
Detector coordinate (in pixels) along the X-direction
               for this reflection. This is often the position where
               the calculated reflection centroid is predicted to occur.

               The detector X-direction is most often along the fast
               changing array index of the 2D diffraction array, while
               the Y-coordinate is along the slow changing array
               index.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_y.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_y
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_y.description:

               the calculated reflection centroid is predicted to occur.

               changing array index of the 2D diffraction array, while
               the Y-coordinate is along the slow changing array
               index.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_rotation_angle.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_rotation_angle
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_rotation_angle.description:

_pdbx_serial_crystallography_data_reduct.description:
For experiments in which samples are provided in a
            continuous stream, the total number of crystal lattices
            that were merged in the final dataset.  Can be
            less than frames_indexed depending on filtering during merging or
	    can be more than frames_indexed if there are multiple lattices.
	    per frame.

_cell.pdbx_esd_method.name: _cell.pdbx_esd_method
_cell.pdbx_esd_method.description: "              How the estimated standard deviation was determined."

enumeration.detail: "ESD from measuring the unit cell using a moving window across many frames"
"Error using a scan varying restraint across the crystal as a whole"
"Standard deviation from a population of many crystals, for example from serial crystallography"
"Errors are derived from a refined fit of the cell parameters to the data and represent the precision of that fit"

_reflns.pdbx_CC_split_method.name: _reflns.pdbx_CC_split_method
_reflns.pdbx_CC_split_method.description:
Method for selecting half datasets used in computing Rsplit,
              CC1/2 and CCstar.

              The following enumerated values are used:

              by_observation: unmerged reflection intensities are randomly
              divided into two half-sets of nearly equal size. As recommended
	      in Karplus PA, Diederichs K. Linking crystallographic model and
	      data quality. Science. 2012;336(6084):1030-1033.

              by_lattice: often used in serial crystallography, crystals are
	      pre-sorted into two half datasets of nearly equal size (such as
	      by odd vs. even crystal number).

"No - Should never be used to represent a protein modification"

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.pcm_id.description: "          An ordinal index for this category."

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.comp_id.description: "          Chemical component identifier for the CCD that contains the modification group."

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.modified_residue_id.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.modified_residue_id
_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.modified_residue_id.description: "          Chemical component identifier for the amino acid residue that is being modified."

enumeration.detail: "Canonical amino acid linked to a peptide residue"
"A diverse range of chemical compounds that form covalent bonds with amino acids. Not covered by any of the other modification categories"
"Chemical modifications which covalently link two amino-acid residues. Excludes reaction intermediates and modifications covered by other modification categories"

"All protein modifications that are well defined into known types, have limited diversity, and are not explicitly covered by any other category. It includes PTMs, PCMs, rare amino acids and unnatural amino acids"

"A diverse range of non-standard polypeptide residues that are not considered to be 'Named protein modifications'. These cannot be practically split into two CCDs, which describe the standard polypeptide residue and the modification group, and are not covered by any of the other modification categories"

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.position.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.position
_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.position.description: "          The position of the modification on the amino acid."

enumeration.detail: "Protein modification occurs on the amino acid side chain"
"Protein modification occurs on the amino acid backbone"
"Protein modification occurs on both the amino acid side chain and backbone"

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.polypeptide_position.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.polypeptide_position
_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.polypeptide_position.description: "          The position of the modification on the polypeptide."

enumeration.detail: "Protein modification can only occur on C-terminus"
"Protein modification can only occur on N-terminus"
"Protein modification can occur in any position of the polypeptide sequence"

_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.comp_id_linking_atom.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.comp_id_linking_atom
_pdbx_chem_comp_pcm.comp_id_linking_atom.description:
The atom on the modification group that covalently links the 
               modification to the residue that is being modified. This is 
               only added when the protein modification is linked and so the 
               amino acid group and the modification group are described by

enumeration.detail: "Canonical amino acid linked to a peptide residue"
"A diverse range of chemical compounds that form covalent bonds with amino acids. Not covered by any of the other modification categories"
"Chemical modifications which covalently link two amino-acid residues. Excludes reaction intermediates and modifications covered by other modification categories"

"All protein modifications that are well defined into known types, have limited diversity, and are not explicitly covered by any other category. It includes PTMs, PCMs, rare amino acids and unnatural amino acids"
"Non-standard covalent chemical linkages directly between two polypeptide residues"

"A diverse range of non-standard polypeptide residues that are not considered to be 'Named protein modifications'. These cannot be practically split into two CCDs, which describe the standard polypeptide residue and the modification group, and are not covered by any of the other modification categories"

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geomet.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geometry_id
_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geomet.description:
This data item is used to group together equivalent geometries coming from different
     software programs. For instance, if one program has an output of 'tetrahedral' and
     another has an output of 'tetrahedron' for the same atom, these two records will
     have the same geometry_id because these two outputs represent the same coordination geometry.

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.comp_i.description:

               category.

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.atom_i.description: "              The identifier for the target atom to which the feature is assigned."

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.number.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.number
_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.number.description: "              The coordination number of the target atom."

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geomet.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geometry_calculated
_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geomet.description:
This data item contains the geometry output from a software program (for example, FindGeo or MetalCoord).
               The geometry derived from observed geometries in atomic coordinate files.

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geomet.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geometry_generic
_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geomet.description:
This data item translates the geometry output from a software program (for example, FindGeo or MetalCoord) into
               a unified naming scheme. As software programs sometimes have different names for the same geometry (for instance,
	       'tetrahedral' and 'tetrahedron' correspond to the same geometry), this data item provides a name for
	       the geometry that is consistent regardless of the provenance to enhance findability. The geometry derived
	       from observed geometries in atomic coordinate files.

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geomet.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geometry_abbr
_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geomet.description:
This data item lists the abbreviated name of the geometry, as specified in the
               _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.descriptor. For instance, square planar
	       is abbreviated to SPL. The geometry derived from observed geometries in atomic coordinate files.

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.proven.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.provenance
_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.proven.description: "              The provenance of the feature assigned to this atom."

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.geometry_id
_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.description:
This data item is a foreign key to _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination.geometry_id. This item maps the
     coordination descriptor to a specific geometry described in the pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination category.

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.comp_id
_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.description:

               category.

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.description: "              The identifier for the target atom to which the feature is assigned."

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.descriptor
_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.description:
This data item contains a descriptor of the coordination environment of
               an atom. The descriptor is derived from observed coordination environments
	       in atomic coordinate files.

_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.name: _pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.provenance
_pdbx_chem_comp_atom_coordination_sphere.description: "              The provenance of the feature assigned to this atom."

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.description:
This data item is used to group together equivalent
               geometries coming from different software programs.
	       For instance, if one program has an output of 'tetrahedral'
               and another has an output of 'tetrahedron' for the same atom,
	       these two records will have the same geometry_id
	       because these two outputs represent the same coordination geometry.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.auth_ato.description:

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.number.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.number
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.number.description: "              The coordination number of the target atom."

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry_calculated
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.description:
This data item contains the geometry output from a software program
               (for example, FindGeo or MetalCoord). The geometry is based on what
	       is observed in the coordinate file.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.software.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.software_remark
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.software.description:
This data item describes the agreement of the
               software-generated geometry with the idealized
               reference geometries. This information is generated by
               the software that generates geometry annotation.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry_generic
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.description:
This data item translates the geometry output from a
               software program (for example, FindGeo or MetalCoord)
               into a unified naming scheme. As software programs
               sometimes have different names for the same geometry
               (for instance, 'tetrahedral' and 'tetrahedron'
               correspond to the same geometry), this data item
               provides a name for the geometry that is consistent
               regardless of the provenance to enhance
               findability. The geometry is based on what is observed
               in the coordinate file.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry_abbr
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry.description:
This data item lists the abbreviated name of the
               geometry, as specified in the
               _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.descriptor. For
               instance, square planar is abbreviated to SPL. The
               geometry derived from observed geometries in atomic
               coordinate files.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.provenan.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.provenance
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.provenan.description: "              The provenance of the feature assigned to this atom."

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.assessme.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.assessment
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.assessme.description:
This data item provides an assessment of the coordination.
               The assessment is based on comparison with the CCD. If coordination
	       is annotated in the CCD, then the coordination in the structure will
	       be compared with the reference(s) in the CCD. If there is a match
	       with the reference CCD, assessment will be annotated as Expected,
	       whereas if there is not a match, assessment will be annotated as Unexpected.
	       If there is no reference in the CCD, assessment will be annotated
	       as 'No reference'.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.i.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.id
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.i.description:
This data item is the parent for
     _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere_order.sphere_id.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere.g.description:
This data item is a foreign key to _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination.geometry_id. This
     item maps the coordination descriptor to a specific geometry described in the
     pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination category.

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere_o.description: "              The identifier of the instance of the STRUCT_ASYM_ID to which this feature applies."

               coordination sphere, which is the encoding of the 3D

_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere_o.name: _pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere_order.symmetry_operation
_pdbx_nonpoly_atom_coordination_sphere_o.description:
The symmetry operator to apply to the specific atom to
               move it into the coordinate spehere.

_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_metal_pi_flag.name: _chem_comp_bond.pdbx_metal_pi_flag
_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_metal_pi_flag.description: "              This data item identifies if this is a bond between a metal and a pi system (alkene, arene, etc)."

_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_metal_coordination_.name: _chem_comp_bond.pdbx_metal_coordination_flag
_chem_comp_bond.pdbx_metal_coordination_.description:
This data item identifies if this is a bond between a metal and its coordinating group, otherwise
               referred to as a dative or coordinate covalent bond. This flag is used for all bonds between
	       metals and non-metals, including metal-pi bonds and all other metal coordination bonds.

_pdbx_depui_status_flags.metal_experimen.name: _pdbx_depui_status_flags.metal_experiment_evidence
_pdbx_depui_status_flags.metal_experimen.description:
This data item will be set to Y if the author provides experimental support for metal features in the DepUI. This
    data item will be set to N if the author does not provide experimental support for metal features in the DepUI.

_diffrn_refln.frame_id.name: _diffrn_refln.frame_id
_diffrn_refln.frame_id.description:
Pointer to _diffrn_scan_frame.frame_id in the DIFFRN_SCAN_FRAME
              category.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_batch_id.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_batch_id
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_batch_id.description: "              Pointer to _pdbx_diffrn_batch.id in the PDBX_DIFFRN_BATCH category."

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_obs_fast.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_obs_fast
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_obs_fast.description:
Detector coordinate (in pixels) along the direction of
               the fast changing array index (of the 2D diffraction
               data) as observed for this reflection. This is often
               the position where the reflection centroid is observed.

               The fast changing array index of the 2D diffraction
               array is often also defined as the detector
               X-coordinate, while the slow changing array index is
               defined as the Y-coordinate. This can vary depending on
               the convention of the detector and the facility,
               especially for multi-panel detectors.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_obs_slow.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_obs_slow
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_obs_slow.description:
Detector coordinate (in pixels) along the direction of
               the slow changing array index (of the 2D diffraction
               data) as observed for this reflection. This is often
               the position where the reflection centroid is observed.

               The slow changing array index of the 2D diffraction
               array is often also defined as the detector
               Y-coordinate, while the fast changing array index is
               defined as the X-coordinate. This can vary depending on
               the convention of the detector and the facility,
               especially for multi-panel detectors.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_calc_fast.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_calc_fast
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_calc_fast.description:
Detector coordinate (in pixels) along the direction of
               the fast changing array index (of the 2D diffraction
               data) for this reflection. This is often the position
               where the calculated reflection is predicted to occur.

               The fast changing array index of the 2D diffraction
               array is often also defined as the detector
               X-coordinate, while the slow changing array index is
               defined as the Y-coordinate. This can vary depending on
               the convention of the detector and the facility,
               especially for multi-panel detectors.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_calc_slow.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_calc_slow
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_detector_calc_slow.description:
Detector coordinate (in pixels) along the direction of
               the slow changing array index (of the 2D diffraction
               data) for this reflection. This is often the position
               where the calculated reflection is predicted to occur.

               The slow changing array index of the 2D diffraction
               array is often also defined as the detector
               Y-coordinate, while the fast changing array index is
               defined as the X-coordinate. This can vary depending on
               the convention of the detector and the facility,
               especially for multi-panel detectors.

_diffrn_refln.pdbx_panel_mapping_id.name: _diffrn_refln.pdbx_panel_mapping_id
_diffrn_refln.pdbx_panel_mapping_id.description:

_pdbx_diffrn_batch.id.description:

               information associated with reflections in the DIFFRN_REFLN
               category.

_pdbx_diffrn_batch.diffrn_id.description:

_pdbx_diffrn_orientation.matrix[1][1].name: _pdbx_diffrn_orientation.matrix[1][1]
_pdbx_diffrn_orientation.matrix[1][1].description:
The [1][1] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the
               orientation of the reciprocal cell with respect to the
               local right-handed coordinate system. See also
               _pdbx_diffrn_orientation.type.

_pdbx_diffrn_orientation.matrix[1][2].description:
The [1][2] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the
               orientation of the reciprocal cell with respect to the
               local right-handed coordinate system. See also
               _pdbx_diffrn_orientation.type.

_pdbx_diffrn_orientation.matrix[1][3].name: _pdbx_diffrn_orientation.matrix[1][3]
_pdbx_diffrn_orientation.matrix[1][3].description:
The [1][3] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the
               orientation of the reciprocal cell with respect to the

The [2][2] element of the 3x3 matrix that defines the

               local right-handed coordinate system. See also

_pdbx_diffrn_orientation.matrix[3][1].name: _pdbx_diffrn_orientation.matrix[3][1]
